title: DiDi Shop Growth Intern
skills: Design and execute A/B tests and localized growth experiments to improve shop sign-up conversion and first-week activation, aiming for measurable uplifts in conversion rate (e.g., +5-15% per experiment).
Analyze platform and merchant data to identify high-impact growth levers, producing weekly dashboards and actionable insights that inform product and marketing priorities.
Coordinate end-to-end merchant acquisition campaigns (online and in-field), managing timelines, creative assets, and partner communication to reach monthly acquisition targets.
Support onboarding and enablement processes by creating playbooks, onboarding flows, and FAQ resources that reduce merchant time-to-first-sale and improve activation KPIs.
Collaborate with product managers and engineers to scope and prioritize feature requests based on merchant feedback and data, documenting requirements and success metrics for iterative releases.
Conduct competitive and market research on local commerce trends and merchant pain points to propose new growth initiatives aligned with DiDi’s regional strategies.
Monitor post-onboarding merchant health and retention signals, proposing targeted re-engagement tactics (promotions, training, co-marketing) to lift 30/60/90-day retention rates.
Currently pursuing a degree in Industrial Engineering, Business, Marketing, Economics, Statistics, or a related field is preferred but not mandatory.
Proficiency in data analysis using Excel
SQL, Python, or BI tools such as Tableau/Looke (nice to hacve)
Familiarity with digital marketing channels (SEM, social, in-app push, CRM) and experience running or supporting performance campaigns with measurable KPIs.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills to work effectively with product, engineering, regional operations, and external merchant partners.
A results-oriented, analytical mindset with attention to detail, demonstrated by prior project work, internships, or coursework that includes measurable outcomes.
Ability to work in a fast-paced, cross-functional environment, prioritize multiple tasks, and adapt plans based on data and business needs.
Cultural sensitivity and local market awareness relevant to DiDi’s operating regions, with the ability to tailor merchant strategies to urban mobility and local commerce dynamics.
description:
About the job

About The Company

DiDi Global Inc. is the world’s leading mobility technology platform. It offers a wide range of app-based services across markets including Asia-Pacific, Latin America and Africa, including ride hailing, taxi hailing, chauffeur, hitch and other forms of shared mobility as well as auto solutions, food delivery, intra-city freight, and financial services.

DiDi provides car owners, drivers, and delivery partners with flexible work and income opportunities. It is committed to collaborating with policymakers, the taxi industry, the automobile industry and the communities to solve the world’s transportation, environmental and employment challenges through the use of AI technology and localized smart transportation innovations. DiDi strives to create better life experiences and greater social value, by building a safe, inclusive and sustainable transportation and local services ecosystem for cities of the future.

For more information, please visit: www.didiglobal.com/news

About The Team/role

As a DiDi Shop Growth Intern, you will support user and merchant strategy efforts for DiDi's in-app ecosystem, directly driving monthly active growth and incremental GMV (gross merchandise value). You will work within the DiDi Shop Growth team to deliver data-driven experiments, localized activation campaigns, and process improvements that increase shop sign-ups, activation rates, and merchant lifetime value, reinforcing DiDi’s strategy to deepen platform monetization and improve user experience across millions of riders and drivers.

In this role, you'll be...


Design and execute A/B tests and localized growth experiments to improve shop sign-up conversion and first-week activation, aiming for measurable uplifts in conversion rate (e.g., +5-15% per experiment).
Analyze platform and merchant data to identify high-impact growth levers, producing weekly dashboards and actionable insights that inform product and marketing priorities.
Coordinate end-to-end merchant acquisition campaigns (online and in-field), managing timelines, creative assets, and partner communication to reach monthly acquisition targets.
Support onboarding and enablement processes by creating playbooks, onboarding flows, and FAQ resources that reduce merchant time-to-first-sale and improve activation KPIs.
Collaborate with product managers and engineers to scope and prioritize feature requests based on merchant feedback and data, documenting requirements and success metrics for iterative releases.
Conduct competitive and market research on local commerce trends and merchant pain points to propose new growth initiatives aligned with DiDi’s regional strategies.
Monitor post-onboarding merchant health and retention signals, proposing targeted re-engagement tactics (promotions, training, co-marketing) to lift 30/60/90-day retention rates.



We're eager to be in touch because you have...


Currently pursuing a degree in Industrial Engineering, Business, Marketing, Economics, Statistics, or a related field is preferred but not mandatory.
Proficiency in data analysis using Excel
SQL, Python, or BI tools such as Tableau/Looke (nice to hacve)
Familiarity with digital marketing channels (SEM, social, in-app push, CRM) and experience running or supporting performance campaigns with measurable KPIs.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills to work effectively with product, engineering, regional operations, and external merchant partners.
A results-oriented, analytical mindset with attention to detail, demonstrated by prior project work, internships, or coursework that includes measurable outcomes.
Ability to work in a fast-paced, cross-functional environment, prioritize multiple tasks, and adapt plans based on data and business needs.
Cultural sensitivity and local market awareness relevant to DiDi’s operating regions, with the ability to tailor merchant strategies to urban mobility and local commerce dynamics.



You'll love working at DiDi because...

We create user value

We strive to always create valuable experiences for our users in everything we do. Our focus is to always innovate new experiences that are safe, pleasant and efficient.

We are data-driven

We are strong believers in making informed decisions, that’s why we are data-driven. We can better navigate the business landscape strategically by analyzing valuable metrics.

Win-win Collaboration

Success is a team sport. When we work to help our partners and colleagues win, we win, too. While keeping everyone's best interest at heart, we communicate with candor and execute with excellence in all we do.

We believe in integrity

Integrity is at the very core of our business. We are people who always want to do the right thing. Our intentions are sincere, we speak our minds and listen to each other.

Growth

We always strive to do better. That means venturing beyond our comfort zones, learning from our mistakes, and helping each other grow.

Diversity and Inclusion

Diversity is one of our biggest strengths. Our differences are what make us distinct. We respect each other and believe in equal opportunities for all.

We are committed to building inclusive and diverse teams

At DiDi, we believe that our differences are our biggest source of strength. That’s why we are committed to promoting equal opportunities to all candidates and employees as an Equal Opportunity Employer.

Employment and advancement decisions at DiDi are always made based on the needs of the position and the qualifications of the candidate. We do not discriminate against any employee or applicant based on their gender, age, sexual orientation, nationality, marital status, pregnancy/maternity, disability, race, religion and beliefs, or any other status protected by applicable laws wherever we operate.

We are committed to building inclusive and diverse teams, and a workplace that is free from discrimination and harassment, because that’s how we create better products and services, make better decisions and better serve the communities we’re a part of.

description:
About the job

At Roche you can show up as yourself, embraced for the unique qualities you bring. Our culture encourages personal expression, open dialogue, and genuine connections, where you are valued, accepted and respected for who you are, allowing you to thrive both personally and professionally. This is how we aim to prevent, stop and cure diseases and ensure everyone has access to healthcare today and for generations to come. Join Roche, where every voice matters.

The Position

Sobre Roche:

Un futuro más saludable. Es lo que nos impulsa a innovar. Avanzar continuamente en la ciencia y garantizar que todos tengan acceso a la atención médica que necesitan hoy y en las generaciones futuras. Crear un mundo en el que todos tengamos más tiempo con las personas que amamos. Eso es lo que nos hace ser Roche.

Oportunidad:

Brindar soporte estratégico y operativo al equipo de marketing en la estructuración, análisis y comunicación de iniciativas relacionadas con el desarrollo y futura comercialización de nuevas moléculas del portafolio. Será un enlace clave en la integración de inputs de diversas áreas, incluyendo Customer Operations, para robustecer propuestas de valor. Desarrollará habilidades de análisis, storytelling estratégico y negociación colaborativa.

Responsabilidades:


Apoyo Estratégico: Colaborar en la elaboración de perfiles estratégicos de molécula y narrativas de posicionamiento
Análisis y Reportes: Contribuir a presentaciones ejecutivas, reportes de avance y visualizaciones de datos clave
Colaboración Interfuncional: Trabajar estrechamente con áreas médicas, acceso, regulatorias, finanzas y Clinical Operations
Alineación de Stakeholders: Desarrollar habilidades de negociación interna para facilitar el alineamiento entre equipos y gestionar conversaciones con diferentes áreas
Gestión de Proyectos: Participar en sesiones interfuncionales, dar seguimiento a entregables y cronogramas, además de actualizar bases de datos y sistemas de gestión del portafolio


Quién eres:


Formación: Estudiante activo(a) de últimos semestres de Mercadotecnia, Administración, Ciencias de la Salud, Ingeniería Biomédica o carreras afines
Intereses: Pasión por la industria farmacéutica y la intersección entre ciencia, estrategia y negocio
Herramientas: Manejo intermedio-avanzado de Excel y PowerPoint (Deseable: Power BI)
Idiomas: Inglés intermedio (capacidad de lectura, escritura y comprensión de materiales técnicos)
Disponibilidad: Medio tiempo por la mañana (mínimo 25 horas por semana)


Competencias Clave:


Alta capacidad de análisis, síntesis de información y pensamiento crítico
Proactividad, responsabilidad y orientación al detalle
Comunicación asertiva, habilidades interpersonales y apertura al aprendizaje constante
Capacidad para trabajar en entornos colaborativos y multifuncionales


Beneficios de reubicación no están disponibles para esta posición.

Who we are

A healthier future drives us to innovate. Together, more than 100’000 employees across the globe are dedicated to advance science, ensuring everyone has access to healthcare today and for generations to come. Our efforts result in more than 26 million people treated with our medicines and over 30 billion tests conducted using our Diagnostics products. We empower each other to explore new possibilities, foster creativity, and keep our ambitions high, so we can deliver life-changing healthcare solutions that make a global impact.

Let’s build a healthier future, together.

Roche is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

title: Performance Marketing Specialist (Google Ads & Landing Pages)
skills: Build and maintain a high volume of landing pages for vehicle sales and parts/service campaigns using the Hercules AI platform.
Create and publish individual landing pages for new used car inventory on a daily basis to keep up with incoming stock.
Set up, manage, and optimize Google Ads and Meta Ads campaigns to drive targeted traffic to the built landing pages.
Connect all landing pages to paid advertising networks and coordinate media strategy across search and social channels to maximize ROI.
Monitor landing page conversion rates, interpret campaign data, and adjust active campaigns to improve ongoing performance.
Track and report on key digital marketing metrics across all active campaigns to maintain visibility on acquisition costs.
Ensure brand consistency, proper messaging alignment, and quality control across all digital marketing assets and public campaigns.
Proven experience managing paid per click (PPC) campaigns on both Google Ads and Meta Ads platforms.
Strong understanding of landing page best practices, user experience, and conversion rate optimization (CRO).
Ability to manage multiple paid campaigns and high volumes of landing pages simultaneously with an exceptional level of organization.
Strong analytical skills with a demonstrated ability to interpret multi-channel campaign data and make quick, data-driven decisions.
Professional English written communication skills combined with a rigorous attention to detail for ad copy and landing page deployment.
Capability to execute technical platform tasks, such as tracking integrations, while reducing the client's direct hands-on involvement.
Permanent work from home
Immediate hiring
description:
About the job

Job Overview




Our client is looking for a Performance Marketing Specialist (Google Ads & Landing Pages) to execute and manage a high-volume digital marketing presence. In this role, you will build an initial batch of 20–100 landing pages for vehicle sales and parts/service campaigns using the Hercules AI platform, followed by creating 3–4 new landing pages daily for incoming used car inventory. This position requires a proactive professional who can seamlessly connect landing pages to Google Ads and Meta Ads for ongoing promotion while ensuring brand consistency and messaging alignment across all digital marketing assets.




Schedule




Monday - Friday, 10:00 AM - 3:00 PM MST/MDT (25 work hours per week)




Responsibilities




Build and maintain a high volume of landing pages for vehicle sales and parts/service campaigns using the Hercules AI platform.
Create and publish individual landing pages for new used car inventory on a daily basis to keep up with incoming stock.
Set up, manage, and optimize Google Ads and Meta Ads campaigns to drive targeted traffic to the built landing pages.
Connect all landing pages to paid advertising networks and coordinate media strategy across search and social channels to maximize ROI.
Monitor landing page conversion rates, interpret campaign data, and adjust active campaigns to improve ongoing performance.
Track and report on key digital marketing metrics across all active campaigns to maintain visibility on acquisition costs.
Ensure brand consistency, proper messaging alignment, and quality control across all digital marketing assets and public campaigns.




Requirements




Proven experience managing paid per click (PPC) campaigns on both Google Ads and Meta Ads platforms.
Strong understanding of landing page best practices, user experience, and conversion rate optimization (CRO).
Ability to manage multiple paid campaigns and high volumes of landing pages simultaneously with an exceptional level of organization.
Strong analytical skills with a demonstrated ability to interpret multi-channel campaign data and make quick, data-driven decisions.
Professional English written communication skills combined with a rigorous attention to detail for ad copy and landing page deployment.
Capability to execute technical platform tasks, such as tracking integrations, while reducing the client's direct hands-on involvement.




Independent Contractor Perks




Permanent work from home
Immediate hiring




Note




Please click the "Apply" button to complete your application, including the assessment questions, technical check, and voice recording. Your hourly pay rate will be established based on your performance in the application process; submissions with all requirements fulfilled will receive priority review.

title: CRM & Email Marketing Analyst
skills: Diseñar journeys automatizados y campañas específicas para promover ancillaries (maletas, asientos, upgrades, etc.) y servicios complementarios después de la reserva.
Identificar tendencias, fricciones y oportunidades mediante el análisis de datos transaccionales y de comportamiento para optimizar la conversión de servicios adicionales.
Ejecutar experimentos para mejorar el performance de las campañas, identificando qué variables impactan más la conversión post booking.
Analiza datos de mercado, tendencias, comportamientos y recopila información de los clientes
Monitorear KPIs como revenue incremental, tasa de conversión por canal y por servicio, aperturas, clics y engagement, y proponer acciones con base en los hallazgos.
Crear y mantener segmentos dinámicos basados en perfil, historial, ruta, canal y tiempo hasta el vuelo para ofrecer mensajes relevantes y oportunos.
Licenciatura concluida en Marketing, Comunicación, Diseño, Mercadotecnia o carreras afines.
Experiencia de 2 a 6 años en CRM, Email Marketing y Marketing Automation.
Experiencia desarrollando estrategias de upselling, campañas automatizadas y customer journeys postcompra.
Conocimiento y manejo de herramientas de segmentación CDP (Customer Data Platform).
Experiencia utilizando herramientas de automatización.
Conocimiento de HTML para la edición y personalización de correos electrónicos.
Dominio avanzado de Microsoft Office, (Excel, Power Point, Word).
Inglés avanzado.
description:
About the job

¡Muchas gracias por tu interés en querer formar parte de nuestra familia #GenteViva!

En Viva nuestro principal propósito es que la gente se divierta, haga negocio y se conecte con sus seres queridos aprovechando la reducción de costo en el transporte aéreo.

¡Estamos seguros que con tu talento, seguiremos volando cada vez más alto!

Como CRM & Email Marketing Analyst, serás responsable de diseñar, ejecutar y optimizar la estrategia de comunicación post booking desde CRM, con foco en la venta de ancillaries y servicios adicionales. Esta posición tiene como objetivo identificar oportunidades de negocio mediante el análisis de datos, segmentación de audiencias y diseño de iniciativas personalizadas que incrementen los ingresos complementarios y mejoren la experiencia del pasajero en su preparación para el viaje a través de los canales de CRM (email, push, in app, etc).

Funciones Principales


Diseñar journeys automatizados y campañas específicas para promover ancillaries (maletas, asientos, upgrades, etc.) y servicios complementarios después de la reserva.
Identificar tendencias, fricciones y oportunidades mediante el análisis de datos transaccionales y de comportamiento para optimizar la conversión de servicios adicionales.
Ejecutar experimentos para mejorar el performance de las campañas, identificando qué variables impactan más la conversión post booking.
Analiza datos de mercado, tendencias, comportamientos y recopila información de los clientes
Monitorear KPIs como revenue incremental, tasa de conversión por canal y por servicio, aperturas, clics y engagement, y proponer acciones con base en los hallazgos.
Crear y mantener segmentos dinámicos basados en perfil, historial, ruta, canal y tiempo hasta el vuelo para ofrecer mensajes relevantes y oportunos.


Requisitos y Habilidades


Licenciatura concluida en Marketing, Comunicación, Diseño, Mercadotecnia o carreras afines.
Experiencia de 2 a 6 años en CRM, Email Marketing y Marketing Automation.
Experiencia desarrollando estrategias de upselling, campañas automatizadas y customer journeys postcompra.
Conocimiento y manejo de herramientas de segmentación CDP (Customer Data Platform).
Experiencia utilizando herramientas de automatización.
Conocimiento de HTML para la edición y personalización de correos electrónicos.
Dominio avanzado de Microsoft Office, (Excel, Power Point, Word).
Inglés avanzado.


¡Súmate a este vuelo y tracemos juntos tu nuevo destino!

title: Marketing Specialist
skills: Executes and participates in general marketing functions that are more routine in nature, require smaller spend budgets and requires minimal contact with clients. Requires oversight with developing approach for more complex marketing functions which include higher thresholds of spend levels.

Participates in collaboration with business development, customers, product management, sales and other business partners in support of client-customized, content-rich marketing collateral, such as proposals, corporate communication, and other marketing related information for marketing functions more routine in nature. Will make recommendations regarding marketing process. Supports the marketing approach within strategy and budget.
Supports the development of strategic marketing plans for existing lines and participates in the interpretation of plans into “big ideas” to extend the company’s brand vision.
Supports the strategic direction, growth, and vision of assigned strategic growth objectives for the segment and manages the execution of programs. Has minimal client contact.
Supports the development of multiple pieces of promotional and advertising materials, such as press releases, social and digital media published on-line via web sites, twitter, etc.

Based on assigned strategic growth initiatives, builds and maintains existing relationships with internal contacts, external communications consultants and/or agencies. Participates in meetings with internal and/or external clients to understand their ongoing strategies and how relationships can be strengthened within the client’s organization.
Supports the development of product promotion and launch strategies.

Beginning to act as a marketing advocate, communications consultant, and/or liaison for business support units and/or operational units within the organization. Supports communications with leaders in those units on specific communications issues and strategic growth initiatives and coordinates appropriate marketing resources to achieve their functional goals.

Recommends measurements for return on investment (ROI) for marketing projects within the specific goals of building and delivering campaigns, such as internal and external communications, product launches, new business units, acquisitions, and RFP/RFI proposals within planned scope, schedule, cost and quality, and trade shows/events. Tracks costs against measurements.
Bachelor's Degree
Relevant Experience or Degree in: Digital Marketing , Advertising ,
Typically Minimum 3 Years Relevant Exp
Prefer marketing service or marketing agency experience. Prefer sales force and email automation experience. Prefer payments industry experience.

Skills / Knowledge - Having wide-ranging experience, uses professional concepts and company objectives to resolve moderately complex issues in creative and effective ways. Having ownership of a sub-function, account or matrix management responsibilities, applies knowledge to meet goals, maintain relationships, propose opportunities to expand the business, and lead matrix teams. Some barriers to entry exist at this level (e.g., dept./peer review).

Job Complexity - Works on complex issues where analysis of situations or data requires an in-depth evaluation of variable factors. Exercises judgment in selecting methods, techniques and evaluation criteria for obtaining results. Networks with key contacts outside own area of expertise. Builds on/Maintains external relationships of assigned accounts.
Supervision - Determines methods and procedures on new assignments and may coordinate activities of other personnel (Team Lead).
Marketing production execution - Broad marketing services and/or print/production
Communication skills - Including communication with internal or external audiences
description:
About the job

Every day, Global Payments makes it possible for millions of people to move money between buyers and sellers using our payments solutions for credit, debit, prepaid and merchant services. Our worldwide team helps over 3 million companies, more than 1,300 financial institutions and over 600 million cardholders grow with confidence and achieve amazing results. We are driven by our passion for success and we are proud to deliver best-in-class payment technology and software solutions. Join our dynamic team and make your mark on the payments technology landscape of tomorrow.

Summary of This Role

Responsible for managing marketing, advertising and promotional activities for various products or channels. Takes steps to measure, enhance, and enrich the position and image of the company through various goals and objectives. Plan and execute day-to-day marketing activities in support of a product, service or channel. Help prepare and adhere to budgets. Oversee creation and delivery of advertising and marketing materials and ensure brand messages are consistent. Collaborate with sales and product to develop strategic partnership activities and implement the execution the identified opportunities.

What Part Will You Play?


Executes and participates in general marketing functions that are more routine in nature, require smaller spend budgets and requires minimal contact with clients. Requires oversight with developing approach for more complex marketing functions which include higher thresholds of spend levels.
Participates in collaboration with business development, customers, product management, sales and other business partners in support of client-customized, content-rich marketing collateral, such as proposals, corporate communication, and other marketing related information for marketing functions more routine in nature. Will make recommendations regarding marketing process. Supports the marketing approach within strategy and budget.
Supports the development of strategic marketing plans for existing lines and participates in the interpretation of plans into “big ideas” to extend the company’s brand vision.
Supports the strategic direction, growth, and vision of assigned strategic growth objectives for the segment and manages the execution of programs. Has minimal client contact.
Supports the development of multiple pieces of promotional and advertising materials, such as press releases, social and digital media published on-line via web sites, twitter, etc.
Based on assigned strategic growth initiatives, builds and maintains existing relationships with internal contacts, external communications consultants and/or agencies. Participates in meetings with internal and/or external clients to understand their ongoing strategies and how relationships can be strengthened within the client’s organization.
Supports the development of product promotion and launch strategies.
Beginning to act as a marketing advocate, communications consultant, and/or liaison for business support units and/or operational units within the organization. Supports communications with leaders in those units on specific communications issues and strategic growth initiatives and coordinates appropriate marketing resources to achieve their functional goals.
Recommends measurements for return on investment (ROI) for marketing projects within the specific goals of building and delivering campaigns, such as internal and external communications, product launches, new business units, acquisitions, and RFP/RFI proposals within planned scope, schedule, cost and quality, and trade shows/events. Tracks costs against measurements.


What Are We Looking For in This Role?

Minimum Qualifications


Bachelor's Degree
Relevant Experience or Degree in: Digital Marketing , Advertising ,
Typically Minimum 3 Years Relevant Exp


Preferred Qualifications


Prefer marketing service or marketing agency experience. Prefer sales force and email automation experience. Prefer payments industry experience.


What Are Our Desired Skills and Capabilities?


Skills / Knowledge - Having wide-ranging experience, uses professional concepts and company objectives to resolve moderately complex issues in creative and effective ways. Having ownership of a sub-function, account or matrix management responsibilities, applies knowledge to meet goals, maintain relationships, propose opportunities to expand the business, and lead matrix teams. Some barriers to entry exist at this level (e.g., dept./peer review).
Job Complexity - Works on complex issues where analysis of situations or data requires an in-depth evaluation of variable factors. Exercises judgment in selecting methods, techniques and evaluation criteria for obtaining results. Networks with key contacts outside own area of expertise. description:
About the job

Estar cursando entre 4 y 5° semestre.

Dominio intermedio de Paquetería Office (Excel, Power Point, Word), Conocimiento general de redes sociales y familiaridad con creación o consumo de contenido digital.

Perfil proactivo, dinámico, multitask, con apertura al trabajo en equipo y enfoque en resultados.







Tenemos un reto para quienes quieran:

Trabajar en una empresa global orgullosamente mexicana.

Dar el siguiente paso en tu carrera profesional junto a un equipo global, productivo y multicultural.
En Cinépolis tenemos el claro objetivo de impulsar la diversidad y la inclusión en todas las dimensiones: género, LGBTQ+, habilidades, etnia y generaciones. Crear experiencias inspiradoras que tocan la vida de las personas, y las historias más importantes son las de nuestros equipos, porque juntos impulsamos nuestro crecimiento de manera responsable y damos forma al futuro cuidando a la comunidad y el medio ambiente.

title: Performance Marketing Specialist
skills: Strategic Planning: Support the team with data and insights to build the company’s acquisition strategy in Mexico.
Plan and execute Digital Marketing strategies: Create and execute the digital marketing plan for paid media in social networks, SEO, SEM and setting up campaigns on tools.
Constant monitoring and development of optimization strategies for paid campaigns on Google, META, ASA, DV360, TikTok and programmatic media to ensure planned KPI's achievement. Google ADS

description:

With 1600+ employees spread across SEA and LATAM, our company has reached unicorn status in 2018, is well funded by prominent VCs and has kept growing at tremendous speed since.

title: SEO Digital Growth & Automation Specialist
description:
About the job

⭐❇️ All applications will be completed on VPM Solutions❇️⭐




To Apply:

Step 1: Click “Apply via LinkedIn” — this will take you to the VPM Solutions page. If you already have a VPM Solutions profile, click “Apply Now.” If not, register and create your Freelancer Profile.

Step 2: Update your profile and make sure your onboarding percentage is at least 80% before applying.

Step 3: Search for the Job Title.

Step 4: Click “Apply Now” and submit your application.

Step 5: Wait for the application result. Keep your account active, as the client or VPM Solutions may reach out if your application passes the screening.




Role Summary

We are seeking for a multi-talented Digital Growth & Automation Specialist to take charge of our in-house marketing and technical operations. This role is designed for a professional who can manage high-level SEO and content strategies while simultaneously handling web development and pioneering AI-driven process integrations.




Key Responsibilities




1. SEO, Content & Social Strategy

· Keyword Intelligence: Perform ongoing keyword research to identify high-intent search terms for property management and real estate investment.

· SEO Optimization: Execute on-page and off-page SEO strategies to maintain and improve Google rankings.

· Content Creation: Research, write, and publish high-quality blog articles that establish WeLease as an industry authority.

· Social Media Repurposing: Systematically repurpose blog content into engaging posts for LinkedIn, Facebook, and Instagram to ensure a consistent social presence.

· Backlink Building: Develop and execute 'white-hat' backlink strategies and digital PR outreach to build domain authority.




2. Web Development & UX Optimization

· Website Management: Maintain, update, and improve the WeLease website (CMS management, plugin updates, security).

· Front-End Development: Design and code high-converting landing pages and UI/UX improvements to increase lead generation.

· Technical SEO: Manage site architecture, schema markup, and speed optimization (Core Web Vitals).




3. AI Process Integration & Automation

· AI Workflow Design: Integrate AI tools (OpenAI, Claude, Gemini) into marketing and admistrative workflows to scale content production and data analysis.

· Process Automation: Use LeadSimple or other products to automate lead routing, CRM updates, and internal notifications, reducing manual entry.

· Innovation: Stay at the forefront of AI developments to identify new ways to streamline property management operations.




Requirements & Qualifications

· 3+ years of experience in Digital Marketing, SEO, or Web Development.

· Proficiency in HTML/CSS and Javascript; experience with CMS platforms (WordPress, Webflow, etc.).

· Expert knowledge of SEO tools (SEMrush, Ahrefs, Google Search Console, GA4).

· Proven experience with automation platforms and LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/ChatGPT/Claude).

· Strong copywriting skills with a focus on professional, long-form content.

· Data-driven mindset with the ability to report on ROI and conversion metrics.

title: Especialista en Marketing de Influencers
skills: Own TikTok Shop Seller Center day to day. Live in the dashboards, know your numbers cold, and make sure performance is healthy across GMV, conversion, and orders.

Make the affiliate program profitable. Track and grow GMV, revenue per creator, sample-to-sales ROI, and commission costs. Protect margin. Every dollar of product and commission has to earn its return.
Build and manage a pipeline of hundreds to thousands of active TikTok Shop affiliates, and keep them actually posting and selling, not just signing up.
Recruit high-converting creators and live sellers who know how to talk about product and drive sales, not just collect free samples.
Go live and sell yourself. You will host TikTok Lives, get on camera, and sell the glasses directly. Then you bring affiliates on, show them how it is done, and coach them to do the same.
Coordinate TikTok Live schedules with strong sellers, secure Spark Ads permissions, and optimize listings and content angles for maximum GMV.
Watch performance weekly. Scale top converters aggressively, and cut low performers fast.
Keep the data honest. No padded numbers, no inflated reporting. Decisions are only as good as the data behind them.
Source Instagram influencers who can actually sell, creators who understand product and know how to drive their audience to buy, not just post a pretty photo.
Run gifting-only and affiliate-only collaborations, and move creators from one gift to repeat posting to consistent affiliate sales.

Rebuild our existing relationships. We have lists of past and dormant influencers. A big part of this role is reigniting those relationships and turning old contacts back into active, converting partners.
Build genuine, consistent relationships so TopFoxx stays top of mind, through thoughtful, regular touchpoints across Instagram and DMs.
Recruit and activate creators on LTK and other affiliate networks (Amazon, affiliate links) who are still actively driving commission-based sales.
Run weekly onboarding calls. You will be on calls regularly, onboarding new influencers, walking them through the program, and getting them excited and set up to post.
Get on calls and close collabs. Be confident and persuasive, build rapport quickly, and convince creators to say yes to working with us, often with gifting only.
Understand creator psychology and motivate influencers to post consistently through relationship, community value, and a clear win for them.
Build the strategy. Bring a clear point of view on where influencer and affiliate marketing is going and how TopFoxx wins, then put it into action.
Plan and execute campaigns tied to product launches, holidays, seasonal moments, and major sales events (BFCM, summer, new drops).
Manage content calendars, gifting coordination, sample sends, and follow-ups so nothing slips.
Build weekly performance reports that connect activity to revenue, and use them to decide what to scale and what to kill.
Create simple SOPs, trackers, and playbooks as you go, so the program is repeatable and scalable as the team grows.
Collaborate with the creative, marketing, and product teams to keep messaging aligned, and step in on urgent launch-related projects when needed.
Stay ahead of trends, new creators, and new affiliate opportunities before competitors do.

Help with wider marketing as needed. With a team this small, marketing is just three people and everyone pitches in. From time to time things will come up across other marketing areas and departments that need a hand. We need someone proactive and solution-oriented who brings energy, jumps in, and helps build this with us rather than sitting idle.
Proactive: You see the gap and fill it. You bring the plan, you do not wait to be told.
A real executor: You move fast, ship daily, and adapt as things change hour to hour.
Analytical and profit-obsessed: You can read a Seller Center dashboard, understand ROI and margin, and turn data into decisions.
Persuasive and outgoing: You love talking to creators, you are comfortable on camera and on calls, and you can sell the partnership and the product.
Relationship-driven: You are a natural connector who builds loyalty and keeps partners coming back.
Organized under pressure: You can juggle hundreds of moving pieces without dropping the ball.
2+ years in influencer or affiliate marketing, preferably in fashion, beauty, lifestyle, or e-commerce / DTC.
Expert, hands-on knowledge of TikTok Shop Seller Center mechanics and affiliate programs.
Proven track record of generating real sales through gifting-only or zero-budget strategies.
Comfort with LTK, Amazon, and other affiliate platforms.
Comfortable on camera and hosting TikTok Lives to sell product directly.
Perfect written and spoken English, and a strong understanding of U.S. culture and digital trends. This is genuinely important.
KPI-driven, highly organized, and able to build simple systems and SOPs as you scale.
Salary of $1,500 to $2,000 per month based on experience, plus performance bonuses tied to affiliate revenue and profitability.
A fully remote role on a fast-moving global team.
Real ownership of the influencer and affiliate function, with a path into leadership as it scales.
PTO including vacation, holidays, sick, personal, and mental health days.
Free TopFoxx products and partner brand perks.
description:
About the job

Affiliate & Influencer Marketing Specialist

TopFoxx | Remote | Full-Time

 

Location: Remote (must work U.S. East Coast hours).

Hours: Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM EST. Hours are flexible. We care about performance, not clocks.

Salary: $1,500 to $2,000 per month based on experience, plus performance bonuses tied to affiliate revenue and profitability.

Language: Perfect written and spoken English is required (this is non-negotiable), along with a deep understanding of U.S. culture, slang, and social media behavior.




About TopFoxx

TopFoxx is a fast-growing, women-owned fashion startup reinventing the sunglasses industry with solution-oriented designs and bold branding. Since 2016, we have grown from a bootstrapped e-commerce brand into a global business serving hundreds of thousands of customers, backed by an engaged community of influencers and creators.

We are not here to follow trends. We create them. Our sunglasses are tangle-free, floatable, foldable, shatter-resistant, and sustainably made, and we are just getting started.

We are a small, lean, founder-led team of fewer than ten people, with only about three of us on marketing. That means there is nowhere to hide and no one to pass the work to. Everyone wears multiple hats, owns real outcomes, and what you build genuinely moves the company. We do not do slow. We move fast, we pivot often, and we only hire A-players who treat the business like their own.




The Role

You will be the main one. This is the person who owns influencer and affiliate marketing end to end at TopFoxx. You are not waiting for a manual or a list of tasks. You are expected to bring the strategy, bring the ideas, and then execute them yourself, fast.

This space changes daily. New creators, new trends, new platform rules. You have to move quickly, test constantly, double down on what converts, and cut what does not. We are not looking for someone who only posts and reports. We are looking for a real executor who is proactive, creative, and obsessed with one thing above all: profitable, converting partnerships.

This is a big opportunity, and we want to be honest with you: it is also real work. We already have a large base of affiliates and creators, but your job is to grow it, activate it, and make it far more profitable. This is not a coast-along role. You will be building, pushing, and problem-solving every single day. For the right person, that is exactly the appeal.

This is a high-ownership role with a direct path into leading the influencer marketing function as it scales.




Why This Is Worth It

Here is the honest pitch. This is not a maintenance job and it is not busywork. Influencer and affiliate marketing is one of the biggest growth levers we have, and we believe this channel can become a multi-million dollar engine for TopFoxx. We are looking for the person who can build that.

We reward outcomes, not hours. If you can show us you can grow this and drive real, profitable revenue, you grow with it. That means a clear path into leadership and compensation that reflects the results you create. The ceiling here is high for someone who actually delivers, and you will be able to point to a business you helped build, not just tasks you completed.

If you want to be a small piece of a big machine, this is not it. If you want real ownership, real upside, and to look back in a year knowing you built something that matters, keep reading.




1. TikTok Shop & Affiliate Program (Your Primary Focus)

This is the heart of the role and where most of your time and energy goes. The big one is affiliate. Your job is to make sure the affiliates are converting, the program is profitable, and the numbers in TikTok Shop Seller Center are strong and trending up.

Own TikTok Shop Seller Center day to day. Live in the dashboards, know your numbers cold, and make sure performance is healthy across GMV, conversion, and orders.
Make the affiliate program profitable. Track and grow GMV, revenue per creator, sample-to-sales ROI, and commission costs. Protect margin. Every dollar of product and commission has to earn its return.
Build and manage a pipeline of hundreds to thousands of active TikTok Shop affiliates, and keep them actually posting and selling, not just signing up.
Recruit high-converting creators and live sellers who know how to talk about product and drive sales, not just collect free samples.
Go live and sell yourself. You will host TikTok Lives, get on camera, and sell the glasses directly. Then you bring affiliates on, show them how it is done, and coach them to do the same.
Coordinate TikTok Live schedules with strong sellers, secure Spark Ads permissions, and optimize listings and content angles for maximum GMV.
Watch performance weekly. Scale top converters aggressively, and cut low performers fast.
Keep the data honest. No padded numbers, no inflated reporting. Decisions are only as good as the data behind them.




2. Instagram Influencers

Instagram is the second pillar. Here we work on gifting and affiliate only, so your skill is finding the right people and getting them to convert without a paid budget.

Source Instagram influencers who can actually sell, creators who understand product and know how to drive their audience to buy, not just post a pretty photo.
Run gifting-only and affiliate-only collaborations, and move creators from one gift to repeat posting to consistent affiliate sales.
Rebuild our existing relationships. We have lists of past and dormant influencers. A big part of this role is reigniting those relationships and turning old contacts back into active, converting partners.
Build genuine, consistent relationships so TopFoxx stays top of mind, through thoughtful, regular touchpoints across Instagram and DMs.




3. LTK, Affiliate Platforms & Onboarding Calls

The third pillar is affiliate-driven platforms like LTK, and just as important, getting on calls with creators. This role is not behind a screen all day. You have to be outgoing and genuinely good at talking to people.

Recruit and activate creators on LTK and other affiliate networks (Amazon, affiliate links) who are still actively driving commission-based sales.
Run weekly onboarding calls. You will be on calls regularly, onboarding new influencers, walking them through the program, and getting them excited and set up to post.
Get on calls and close collabs. Be confident and persuasive, build rapport quickly, and convince creators to say yes to working with us, often with gifting only.
Understand creator psychology and motivate influencers to post consistently through relationship, community value, and a clear win for them.




4. Strategy, Reporting & Full Influencer Marketing Scope

Because you own this function, you also handle everything a strong e-commerce influencer marketing lead would handle. Study what bigger e-commerce brands do, then adapt the best of it to a lean, fast-moving team.

Build the strategy. Bring a clear point of view on where influencer and affiliate marketing is going and how TopFoxx wins, then put it into action.
Plan and execute campaigns tied to product launches, holidays, seasonal moments, and major sales events (BFCM, summer, new drops).
Manage content calendars, gifting coordination, sample sends, and follow-ups so nothing slips.
Build weekly performance reports that connect activity to revenue, and use them to decide what to scale and what to kill.
Create simple SOPs, trackers, and playbooks as you go, so the program is repeatable and scalable as the team grows.
Collaborate with the creative, marketing, and product teams to keep messaging aligned, and step in on urgent launch-related projects when needed.
Stay ahead of trends, new creators, and new affiliate opportunities before competitors do.
Help with wider marketing as needed. With a team this small, marketing is just three people and everyone pitches in. From time to time things will come up across other marketing areas and departments that need a hand. We need someone proactive and solution-oriented who brings energy, jumps in, and helps build this with us rather than sitting idle.




Who You Are

Proactive: You see the gap and fill it. You bring the plan, you do not wait to be told.
A real executor: You move fast, ship daily, and adapt as things change hour to hour.
Analytical and profit-obsessed: You can read a Seller Center dashboard, understand ROI and margin, and turn data into decisions.
Persuasive and outgoing: You love talking to creators, you are comfortable on camera and on calls, and you can sell the partnership and the product.
Relationship-driven: You are a natural connector who builds loyalty and keeps partners coming back.
Organized under pressure: You can juggle hundreds of moving pieces without dropping the ball.




Required Experience & Skills

2+ years in influencer or affiliate marketing, preferably in fashion, beauty, lifestyle, or e-commerce / DTC.
Expert, hands-on knowledge of TikTok Shop Seller Center mechanics and affiliate programs.
Proven track record of generating real sales through gifting-only or zero-budget strategies.
Comfort with LTK, Amazon, and other affiliate platforms.
Comfortable on camera and hosting TikTok Lives to sell product directly.
Perfect written and spoken English, and a strong understanding of U.S. culture and digital trends. This is genuinely important.
KPI-driven, highly organized, and able to build simple systems and SOPs as you scale.




What You Get

Salary of $1,500 to $2,000 per month based on experience, plus performance bonuses tied to affiliate revenue and profitability.
A fully remote role on a fast-moving global team.
Real ownership of the influencer and affiliate function, with a path into leadership as it scales.
PTO including vacation, holidays, sick, personal, and mental health days.
Free TopFoxx products and partner brand perks.

title: Driver Growth Operations Intern
skills: Funnel Diagnostics: Track regional user acquisition-to-activation funnels, isolating conversion drop-offs and channel efficiencies to optimize operational resources.

Cohort Analytics: Maintain performance models evaluating user lifecycles and maturity lags, running precise comparisons between treatment and control populations to isolate incremental performance gains.
Capacity Planning: Model supply capacity across regional clusters to forecast operational runway and proactively flag localized gaps before they stall expansion.
Data Pipeline Auditing: Be the primary gatekeeper for data integrity, identifying and resolving systemic anomalies between operational tools and centralized data warehouses.
Execution Tracking: Build simple compliance frameworks to evaluate field implementation quality, system utilization, and operational workflow adherence.
Process Automation: Configure automated lifecycle triggers within internal systems and leverage AI tools to eliminate manual reporting or operating loops.
Education: Enrolled in 5th–8th semester of Actuarial Science, Engineering, Computer Science, Economics, or a similarly ultra-quantitative field.
Technical Programming: Strong SQL foundations paired with data processing skills in Python (Pandas), R, or similar frameworks to manipulate and model complex datasets independently.
Automation Mindset: Proactive problem-solver with a strong interest in using tech-driven solutions and AI tools to optimize workflow efficiency.
Advanced Spreadsheets: Mastery of Excel/Google Sheets (complex logic, structured modeling) to build operational trackers from scratch.
Execution Rigor: Highly organized with the disciplined time management required to balance university schedules with fast-paced corporate sprints.
Communication: Professional fluency in both Spanish and English, with the ability to summarize raw trends into clear operational insights.
description:
About the job

About The Company

DiDi Global Inc. is the world’s leading mobility technology platform. It offers a wide range of app-based services across markets including Asia-Pacific, Latin America and Africa, including ride hailing, taxi hailing, chauffeur, hitch and other forms of shared mobility as well as auto solutions, food delivery, intra-city freight, and financial services.

DiDi provides car owners, drivers, and delivery partners with flexible work and income opportunities. It is committed to collaborating with policymakers, the taxi industry, the automobile industry and the communities to solve the world’s transportation, environmental and employment challenges through the use of AI technology and localized smart transportation innovations. DiDi strives to create better life experiences and greater social value, by building a safe, inclusive and sustainable transportation and local services ecosystem for cities of the future.

For more information, please visit: www.didiglobal.com/news

About The Team/role

Being part of the Driver Growth Operations team will give you the unique opportunity to build and optimize the supply engine of the leading mobility platform. In this role, you will help transform Driver Management into a high-impact, scalable supply capabilities model across Spanish-Speaking LatAm. Our team builds data-driven acquisition and engagement systems that structurally improve user lifecycles, boost lifetime value, and enhance platform preference.

In this role, you'll be...

As a Driver Growth Operations Intern you’ll be the analytical core for our primary scale and quality workstreams. You will maintain and optimize complex automated dashboards, monitor behavioral data patterns, and surface actionable insights needed to scale our regional operations. If you want to combine technical data skills with real-world marketplace logistics in a fast-paced corporate environment, this role offers an ideal proving ground. Your core responsibilities will include:


Funnel Diagnostics: Track regional user acquisition-to-activation funnels, isolating conversion drop-offs and channel efficiencies to optimize operational resources.
Cohort Analytics: Maintain performance models evaluating user lifecycles and maturity lags, running precise comparisons between treatment and control populations to isolate incremental performance gains.
Capacity Planning: Model supply capacity across regional clusters to forecast operational runway and proactively flag localized gaps before they stall expansion.
Data Pipeline Auditing: Be the primary gatekeeper for data integrity, identifying and resolving systemic anomalies between operational tools and centralized data warehouses.
Execution Tracking: Build simple compliance frameworks to evaluate field implementation quality, system utilization, and operational workflow adherence.
Process Automation: Configure automated lifecycle triggers within internal systems and leverage AI tools to eliminate manual reporting or operating loops.



We're eager to be in touch because you have...


Education: Enrolled in 5th–8th semester of Actuarial Science, Engineering, Computer Science, Economics, or a similarly ultra-quantitative field.
Technical Programming: Strong SQL foundations paired with data processing skills in Python (Pandas), R, or similar frameworks to manipulate and model complex datasets independently.
Automation Mindset: Proactive problem-solver with a strong interest in using tech-driven solutions and AI tools to optimize workflow efficiency.
Advanced Spreadsheets: Mastery of Excel/Google Sheets (complex logic, structured modeling) to build operational trackers from scratch.
Execution Rigor: Highly organized with the disciplined time management required to balance university schedules with fast-paced corporate sprints.
Communication: Professional fluency in both Spanish and English, with the ability to summarize raw trends into clear operational insights.



You'll love working at DiDi because...

We create user value

We strive to always create valuable experiences for our users in everything we do. Our focus is to always innovate new experiences that are safe, pleasant and efficient.

We are data-driven

We are strong believers in making informed decisions, that’s why we are data-driven. We can better navigate the business landscape strategically by analyzing valuable metrics.

Win-win Collaboration

Success is a team sport. When we work to help our partners and colleagues win, we win, too. While keeping everyone's best interest at heart, we communicate with candor and execute with excellence in all we do.

We believe in integrity

Integrity is at the very core of our business. We are people who always want to do the right thing. Our intentions are sincere, we speak our minds and listen to each other.

Growth

We always strive to do better. That means venturing beyond our comfort zones, learning from our mistakes, and helping each other grow.

Diversity and Inclusion

Diversity is one of our biggest strengths. Our differences are what make us distinct. We respect each other and believe in equal opportunities for all.

We are committed to building inclusive and diverse teams

At DiDi, we believe that our differences are our biggest source of strength. That’s why we are committed to promoting equal opportunities to all candidates and employees as an Equal Opportunity Employer.

Employment and advancement decisions at DiDi are always made based on the needs of the position and the qualifications of the candidate. We do not discriminate against any employee or applicant based on their gender, age, sexual orientation, nationality, marital status, pregnancy/maternity, disability, race, religion and beliefs, or any other status protected by applicable laws wherever we operate.

We are committed to building inclusive and diverse teams, and a workplace that is free from discrimination and harassment, because that’s how we create better products and services, make better decisions and better serve the communities we’re a part of.

skills: Own Social Media CX operational processes end-to-end, from response flows to continuous optimization.

Business-level English, able to work with internal and regional stakeholders.
Familiarity with social care tools used for CX operations (e.g., Sprinklr or similar).

title: User Growth Operations Sr. Analyst
skills: Support the design and execution of integrated growth campaigns across different categories, seasonalities and user segments.
Collaborate closely with Marketing, Product and Commercial teams to implement pricing, promotion and communication strategies.
Leverage in-app surfaces such as banners, pop-ups, push notifications and CRM channels to drive user acquisition, engagement and retention.
Analyze campaign performance using data and key metrics to evaluate results, identify opportunities and recommend optimizations.
Translate data insights into actionable learnings to improve future campaigns and overall growth performance.
Contribute to creative ideation and campaign iteration based on data, user behavior and experiment results.
Execute operational tasks with high attention to detail in a fast-paced environment.
3–5 years of experience in analytics, growth, user operations or similar roles within tech, marketplaces or fast-paced environments.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret data, identify trends and turn insights into actionable recommendations.
Hands-on experience using data analysis tools, including SQL, to analyze campaign performance and business results.
Experience supporting or executing growth initiatives related to pricing, promotions, campaigns or user communications.
Familiarity with CRM tools and in-app communication channels such as push notifications, banners or pop-ups.
High attention to detail and strong execution skills, with the ability to manage multiple initiatives simultaneously.
A proactive mindset, curiosity to learn and comfort working in dynamic, cross-functional teams.
Fluency in English as a working language.

title: Marketing Manager Growth & Product
skills: Leading marketing growth and product strategy connecting the business ambition with the brand vision based on analytical and strategic thinking, including development of goals, approach, strategic definition and plan.
Bring innovative marketing ideas to the table, and best in class strategic initiatives, obtaining buy-in and efficiently implementing them at scale through marketing programs.
Business driven mindset defining strategic plans and actions to increase market share.
Use internal and external data to find relevant insights that transform into effective Brand insights, campaigns and content.
Drive research recommendations, and discussions to find sources of growth around the product, user value proposition and market expansion.
Translate high-level strategy and positioning into concrete deliverables (campaign blueprints, messaging frameworks, value propositions, etc.)
Develop marketing campaigns; providing input into media plans, key messaging RTBs and strategic briefs.
Develop campaign reporting crossing business results with brand results to find sources of growth for user value creation based on learnings and optimizations.
Develop enablement materials (Toolkits, playbooks) to scale new developments/learnings and help drive brand consistency and market penetration.
Remain fully informed on market trends and marketing brand campaigns.
6+ years of relevant experience developing marketing strategy and plan at a regional level.
Strong stakeholder management and leadership influencing teams.
Knowledge in product marketing, inapp campaigns and go to marketing strategies.
Fast learner; ability to manage competing priorities and operate effectively in a matrixed organization / fast-paced environment
Understands the “why” and applies critical thinking and strategic reasoning to decision making.
Experience in Brand and business KPIs analysis
Experience with in-app and 360 campaigns
Be able to work under constant deadline pressure and manage multiple projects.
You are fluent in English.

title: Marketing Specialist
skills: Contractor check-in cadence Goal: every 2 to 3 business days per active contractor
Availability tracking: Maintain regularly updated visibility into contractor capacity
Work order updates: Call contractors directly when needed to keep their day on track
Sourcing replacements: Begin sourcing an alternative as soon as a contractor is unavailable
Mentoring Guide contractors and vendors on how and to what extent to bid a project
Documentation Log all contractor communication in the appropriate portal within 24 hours
Performance tracking Monitor each contractor across quality, timeliness, and client feedback on an ongoing basis
Skill matching Assign work to the best-suited contractor; never repeat a poor-quality match on the same work type regardless of availability
Performance flags Document and flag underperforming contractors to the Director of Operations within 5 business days.
Probation / removal Support or initiate removal conversations for contractors who do not meet standards.
P&P accountability Hold vendors accountable to the Best Practices P&P Manual; identify when leadership involvement is needed
NOEs: Actioned within required timeframe; escalated to Director of Operations before the deadline if at risk
Cancellations Managed proactively with clear contractor and client communication
Reassignments Initiated promptly when a contractor cannot fulfill an order
Escalation timing Raised to Director of Operations with enough lead time to allow intervention
Aged work order review All aged orders reviewed and actioned every business day Portal accuracy All orders updated correctly in client portals by end of each business day
Bid entry Assist with bid entry for aged work orders when doing so prevents further delay.
Portal scheduling All scheduling communicated and logged correctly in client portals
Response time Target: within 4 business hours for routine inquiries; urgent concerns same-day
Update quality All client updates must be accurate, meaningful, and solution-oriented
Escalations Handle within your authority level; escalate to Director of Operations when outside that scope.
Pricing / commitments Any communication involving pricing, commitments, or escalations must be reviewed with the Director of Operations before sending
description:
About the job

⭐❇️ All applications will be completed on VPM Solutions❇️⭐




To Apply:

Step 1: Click “Apply via LinkedIn” — this will take you to the VPM Solutions page. If you already have a VPM Solutions profile, click “Apply Now.” If not, register and create your Freelancer Profile.

Step 2: Update your profile and make sure your onboarding percentage is at least 80% before applying.

Step 3: Search for the Job Title.

Step 4: Click “Apply Now” and submit your application.

Step 5: Wait for the application result. Keep your account active, as the client or VPM Solutions may reach out if your application passes the screening.







Job Role




This document defines the specific, measurable expectations for the Market Manager role at Shamrock. It exists to create clarity on what success looks like, provide a shared framework for regular coaching conversations, and give both leadership and the Market Manager a common standard to work from.




Both sides share responsibility for making this work: leadership commits to providing the coaching, tools, escalation support, and reasonable workload that make these standards achievable. The Market Manager commits to operating with ownership, proactivity, and professionalism.




1. Contractor Communication and Availability




Contractors are the operational foundation of each market. Keeping them engaged, informed, and

correctly assigned is one of the most important functions of this role.




Check-In Requirements




Contractor check-in cadence Goal: every 2 to 3 business days per active contractor
Availability tracking: Maintain regularly updated visibility into contractor capacity
Work order updates: Call contractors directly when needed to keep their day on track
Sourcing replacements: Begin sourcing an alternative as soon as a contractor is unavailable
Mentoring Guide contractors and vendors on how and to what extent to bid a project
Documentation Log all contractor communication in the appropriate portal within 24 hours




2. Contractor Performance and Accountability




Shamrock's reputation depends on the quality of work delivered. The Market Manager is responsible for knowing who performs at standard, who does not, and acting accordingly.




Performance Standards




Performance tracking Monitor each contractor across quality, timeliness, and client feedback on an ongoing basis
Skill matching Assign work to the best-suited contractor; never repeat a poor-quality match on the same work type regardless of availability
Performance flags Document and flag underperforming contractors to the Director of Operations within 5 business days.
Probation / removal Support or initiate removal conversations for contractors who do not meet standards.
P&P accountability Hold vendors accountable to the Best Practices P&P Manual; identify when leadership involvement is needed




3. SLA Compliance




SLA performance is a direct measure of operational health. The Market Manager is responsible for proactively managing SLA risk, not reacting after a deadline has passed.




NOEs: Actioned within required timeframe; escalated to Director of Operations before the deadline if at risk
Cancellations Managed proactively with clear contractor and client communication
Reassignments Initiated promptly when a contractor cannot fulfill an order
Escalation timing Raised to Director of Operations with enough lead time to allow intervention




4. Work Order Management




All open work orders should reflect documented follow-up activity appropriate to their current stage. The Market Manager is responsible for proactively driving each work order toward resolution.




Daily Requirements




Aged work order review All aged orders reviewed and actioned every business day Portal accuracy All orders updated correctly in client portals by end of each business day
Bid entry Assist with bid entry for aged work orders when doing so prevents further delay.
Portal scheduling All scheduling communicated and logged correctly in client portals




5. Client Communication




Clients should feel informed, not surprised. The Market Manager is the day-to-day operational voice to clients. Professionalism, responsiveness, and solution-oriented communication define the standard.




Response time Target: within 4 business hours for routine inquiries; urgent concerns same-day
Update quality All client updates must be accurate, meaningful, and solution-oriented
Escalations Handle within your authority level; escalate to Director of Operations when outside that scope.
Pricing / commitments Any communication involving pricing, commitments, or escalations must be reviewed with the Director of Operations before sending




6. Reporting and Shared Accountability




Weekly Activity Report — Due Every Friday

• Aged work orders: status and actions taken on all open aged orders

• Contractor status: availability changes, performance concerns, or removal discussions

• SLA risks: any active or anticipated concerns

• Client escalations: any open client issues

• Wins: notable positive outcomes from the week

Weekly 1:1 with Leadership

• Held weekly at a consistent, scheduled time

• Market Manager comes prepared with the activity report and a clear agenda

• This is a coaching and support conversation, not just a status update

Leadership Commitments

• Respond to escalations requiring Director review within 4 business hours

• Address MM blockers raised in the weekly 1:1 within one business day

• Conduct quarterly reviews of workload volume to ensure expectations remain reasonable

• Communicate clearly when responsibilities shift between teams

skills: You will be paid in USD (bi-monthly: every 15th and 30th)
Paid Time Off in accordance with company policy
Observance of Holidays per company guidelines
100% remote setup so you can work wherever you're most productive
Direct ownership over email and retention execution for a design-led DTC brand
Clear briefs, defined feedback loops, and a founder who is actively involved in brand direction
Draft email copy and assemble email designs in Klaviyo based on internal briefs
Build and schedule weekly campaigns aligned to the promotional calendar
Set up segmentation, exclusions, and send logic including recent purchasers, VIP, and lapsed windows
QA every send including links, personalization, dynamic blocks, mobile rendering, UTMs, and deliverability checks
Maintain a clean rolling campaign calendar covering four to six weeks at a time

Audit, build, and continuously optimize core flows including welcome series, browse abandon, cart and checkout abandon, post-purchase education and cross-sell, review request, winback, VIP, and back-in-stock or price drop flows where applicable
Maintain clear flow architecture and naming conventions
Run A/B tests on timing, subject lines, content blocks, and offer logic
Ensure flows reflect promotional guardrails and stay consistent with brand tone
Maintain key segments including new subscribers, first-time buyers, repeat buyers, VIP, lapsed windows, and category interest
Own suppression logic and exclusions to avoid over-emailing and protect customer experience
Support personalization and dynamic content logic across Klaviyo
Monitor deliverability and list health including bounce rates, spam complaints, and open and click trends
Maintain list hygiene, suppression, and sunsetting programs
Recommend cadence adjustments to protect long-term deliverability
Deliver a weekly one-page report covering campaign results, flow results, key learnings, and next actions
Provide a monthly retention review covering flow contribution, segment performance, and a testing roadmap
Maintain a testing log documenting what was tested, results, and decisions made
3 to 5+ years of email and lifecycle marketing experience in ecommerce or DTC

Ability to draft clean, on-brand email copy and build polished email layouts directly in Klaviyo
High QA discipline and operational rigor: you catch errors before they send, not after
Comfortable interpreting performance data and proposing clear next actions

Sensitivity to premium and luxury-adjacent brand tone and aesthetics
SMS experience in Klaviyo SMS, Attentive, or Postscript

Experience building retention calendars around promotional guardrails

Your updated resume
A short Loom video (1 to 2 minutes) walking through a Klaviyo flow or campaign you built, what the brief was, and what the result looked like in terms of performance
description:
About the job

We're looking for a hands-on Email Marketing Specialist to own day-to-day retention execution in Klaviyo for a premium DTC accessories brand. Internal strategy and creative direction will be provided by the team. Your job is to turn those briefs into polished, on-brand emails and a retention system that drives repeat purchase.




This is an execution role, not a strategy role. You will draft copy, build designs in Klaviyo, implement and optimize lifecycle flows, execute campaigns, manage segmentation, maintain deliverability health, and run a consistent testing and reporting rhythm every single week. If you are a hands-on Klaviyo operator who can work within a brand voice that is warm, editorial, and premium without being stiff, this role is a strong fit.




Why You'll Want to Join

You will be paid in USD (bi-monthly: every 15th and 30th)
Paid Time Off in accordance with company policy
Observance of Holidays per company guidelines
100% remote setup so you can work wherever you're most productive
Direct ownership over email and retention execution for a design-led DTC brand
Clear briefs, defined feedback loops, and a founder who is actively involved in brand direction




What You'll Work On




Email Campaign Execution

Draft email copy and assemble email designs in Klaviyo based on internal briefs
Build and schedule weekly campaigns aligned to the promotional calendar
Set up segmentation, exclusions, and send logic including recent purchasers, VIP, and lapsed windows
QA every send including links, personalization, dynamic blocks, mobile rendering, UTMs, and deliverability checks
Maintain a clean rolling campaign calendar covering four to six weeks at a time




Lifecycle Flow Ownership

Audit, build, and continuously optimize core flows including welcome series, browse abandon, cart and checkout abandon, post-purchase education and cross-sell, review request, winback, VIP, and back-in-stock or price drop flows where applicable
Maintain clear flow architecture and naming conventions
Run A/B tests on timing, subject lines, content blocks, and offer logic
Ensure flows reflect promotional guardrails and stay consistent with brand tone




Segmentation and Retention Logic

Maintain key segments including new subscribers, first-time buyers, repeat buyers, VIP, lapsed windows, and category interest
Own suppression logic and exclusions to avoid over-emailing and protect customer experience
Support personalization and dynamic content logic across Klaviyo




Deliverability and List Health

Monitor deliverability and list health including bounce rates, spam complaints, and open and click trends
Maintain list hygiene, suppression, and sunsetting programs
Recommend cadence adjustments to protect long-term deliverability




Reporting and Optimization

Deliver a weekly one-page report covering campaign results, flow results, key learnings, and next actions
Provide a monthly retention review covering flow contribution, segment performance, and a testing roadmap
Maintain a testing log documenting what was tested, results, and decisions made




What You Bring

3 to 5+ years of email and lifecycle marketing experience in ecommerce or DTC
Strong Klaviyo mastery including flows, segmentation, dynamic content, reporting, and template builds
Ability to draft clean, on-brand email copy and build polished email layouts directly in Klaviyo
High QA discipline and operational rigor: you catch errors before they send, not after
Comfortable interpreting performance data and proposing clear next actions
Clear, reliable communicator who can execute within a fast weekly cadence
Sensitivity to premium and luxury-adjacent brand tone and aesthetics




Nice to Have

SMS experience in Klaviyo SMS, Attentive, or Postscript
Basic HTML familiarity for email module edits
Experience building retention calendars around promotional guardrails
Background in fashion, accessories, or lifestyle DTC brands




How to Apply

Please include:

Your updated resume
A short Loom video (1 to 2 minutes) walking through a Klaviyo flow or campaign you built, what the brief was, and what the result looked like in terms of performance




Only candidates who submit a Loom video will be moved to the next step of the hiring process.




If you are a precise, reliable Klaviyo operator who can bring brand briefs to life in the inbox and keep a retention system running cleanly week after week, this role gives you the ownership and the creative context to do your best work for a brand that takes quality seriously.

title: Digital Acquisition & Performance Specialist

Monitorear la inversión diaria/semanal y asegurar el uso eficiente del presupuesto asignado.
Gestionar campañas de Search, Display, Performance Max y remarketing.
Realizar research de keywords, análisis de términos de búsqueda y optimización de keywords negativas.
Mejorar la calidad del tráfico mediante ajustes de intención de búsqueda, pujas, extensiones y landings.
Identificar oportunidades de campañas defensivas y ofensivas frente a competidores o categorías clave.
Crear campañas B2B enfocadas en audiencias estratégicas por cargo, industria, empresa, seniority y país.
Manejar campañas de generación de demanda, lead generation forms, tráfico a landing pages y remarketing.

Evaluar la calidad de leads provenientes de Meta y proponer ajustes para mejorar intención, segmentación y conversión.
Coordinar con diseño/contenido para desarrollar creatividades adaptadas a cada audiencia y etapa del funnel.
