Coordinarse con el equipo comercial para revisar la calidad de leads, citas generadas y oportunidades creadas.
Analizar el desempeño por fuente, país, campaña, anuncio y landing page.
Proponer mejoras en formularios, landings y journeys de conversión.
Apoyar en la definición de criterios de lead quality, MQLs y oportunidades comerciales.
Crear reportes constantes de performance por canal, campaña, país y línea de negocio.
Analizar métricas clave: leads, CPL, citas, costo por cita, conversión a oportunidad, inversión, ROAS y CAC.
Identificar insights accionables y presentar recomendaciones para optimizar inversión y resultados.
Trabajar con herramientas de medición como GA4, Google Tag Manager, Looker Studio, CRM y plataformas de anuncios.
Apoyar la estrategia de adquisición orgánica mediante análisis de keywords, intención de búsqueda y oportunidades SEO.
Colaborar con el equipo de contenido para identificar temas con potencial de conversión.
Apoyar iniciativas de visibilidad en buscadores, AI Overviews, rich results y contenido orientado a demanda.
Google Ads
Meta Ads
LinkedIn Ads
Liderar la ejecución táctica y optimización de campañas digitales pagadas para generación de demanda.
Asegurar que las campañas digitales contribuyan al cumplimiento de objetivos comerciales: leads calificados, citas, oportunidades y clientes.
Administrar el presupuesto asignado a medios digitales, priorizando eficiencia, conversión y calidad del lead.
Trabajar de forma coordinada con marketing, ventas, diseño, contenido y operaciones comerciales.
Dar seguimiento al desempeño del funnel digital, desde la campaña hasta el CRM.
Proponer mejoras constantes en audiencias, mensajes, creatividades, landings, formularios y journeys de conversión.
Detectar oportunidades de crecimiento por canal, país, producto, industria y buyer persona.
Presentar resultados, insights y recomendaciones accionables para mejorar el performance de adquisición.
Mantener actualizadas las mejores prácticas de paid media, adquisición digital, automatización, tracking y performance B2B.
description:
About the job

Objetivo del puesto:

 

Buscamos un/a Digital Acquisition & Performance Specialist responsable de planear, ejecutar, optimizar y medir campañas digitales de adquisición enfocadas en generación de leads calificados y oportunidades comerciales.

La persona será responsable principalmente del manejo de campañas pagadas en LinkedIn Ads, Meta Ads —Facebook/Instagram— y Google Ads, asegurando que la inversión digital se traduzca en tráfico de calidad, leads relevantes, citas comerciales y oportunidades para el equipo de ventas.

Este rol será clave para fortalecer la estrategia de adquisición digital, mejorar la eficiencia del funnel y escalar los canales de paid media con foco en performance, conversión y calidad de leads.

 Conocimientos indispensables:

Paid Media & Performance

Planear, ejecutar, monitorear y optimizar campañas pagadas en LinkedIn Ads, Meta Ads y Google Ads.
Administrar campañas orientadas a generación de leads, tráfico calificado, formularios, conversiones y remarketing.
Definir audiencias, segmentaciones, copies, creatividades, presupuestos y estructuras de campaña por país, producto, industria o buyer persona.
Optimizar campañas con base en métricas como CPL, CPC, CTR, tasa de conversión, costo por cita y calidad del lead.
Implementar pruebas A/B de mensajes, creatividades, audiencias, landings y formatos de anuncio.
Detectar oportunidades de optimización por canal, país, industria y etapa del funnel.
Monitorear la inversión diaria/semanal y asegurar el uso eficiente del presupuesto asignado.

Google Ads & SEM

Gestionar campañas de Search, Display, Performance Max y remarketing.
Realizar research de keywords, análisis de términos de búsqueda y optimización de keywords negativas.
Mejorar la calidad del tráfico mediante ajustes de intención de búsqueda, pujas, extensiones y landings.
Identificar oportunidades de campañas defensivas y ofensivas frente a competidores o categorías clave.

LinkedIn Ads

Crear campañas B2B enfocadas en audiencias estratégicas por cargo, industria, empresa, seniority y país.
Manejar campañas de generación de demanda, lead generation forms, tráfico a landing pages y remarketing.

Meta Ads

Gestionar campañas en Facebook e Instagram para awareness, tráfico, leads y remarketing.
Evaluar la calidad de leads provenientes de Meta y proponer ajustes para mejorar intención, segmentación y conversión.
Coordinar con diseño/contenido para desarrollar creatividades adaptadas a cada audiencia y etapa del funnel.

Funnel, CRM & Sales Alignment

Asegurar una correcta medición del funnel desde campaña hasta CRM.
Coordinarse con el equipo comercial para revisar la calidad de leads, citas generadas y oportunidades creadas.
Analizar el desempeño por fuente, país, campaña, anuncio y landing page.
Proponer mejoras en formularios, landings y journeys de conversión.
Apoyar en la definición de criterios de lead quality, MQLs y oportunidades comerciales.

Analytics & Reporting

Crear reportes constantes de performance por canal, campaña, país y línea de negocio.
Analizar métricas clave: leads, CPL, citas, costo por cita, conversión a oportunidad, inversión, ROAS y CAC.
Identificar insights accionables y presentar recomendaciones para optimizar inversión y resultados.
Trabajar con herramientas de medición como GA4, Google Tag Manager, Looker Studio, CRM y plataformas de anuncios.

SEO, AEO & Digital Acquisition

Apoyar la estrategia de adquisición orgánica mediante análisis de keywords, intención de búsqueda y oportunidades SEO.
Colaborar con el equipo de contenido para identificar temas con potencial de conversión.
Apoyar iniciativas de visibilidad en buscadores, AI Overviews, rich results y contenido orientado a demanda.

 

Experiencia de 2 a 4 años en performance marketing, paid media, digital acquisition o growth marketing. 

Experiencia comprobable manejando campañas en: 

Google Ads 
Meta Ads 
LinkedIn Ads 

Experiencia en campañas B2B, SaaS, HR Tech, consultoría, tecnología o servicios profesionales. 

Conocimiento sólido de funnels de adquisición, generación de leads y optimización de conversiones. 

Experiencia trabajando con CRM, idealmente Pipedrive, HubSpot, Salesforce o similares. 

Conocimiento de GA4, Google Tag Manager y Looker Studio. 

Capacidad analítica para interpretar datos y convertirlos en recomendaciones accionables. 

Experiencia optimizando landings, formularios y journeys digitales. 

Inglés intermedio o avanzado deseable.

 

Responsabilidades:

Asegurar que las campañas digitales contribuyan al cumplimiento de objetivos comerciales: leads calificados, citas, oportunidades y clientes.
Administrar el presupuesto asignado a medios digitales, priorizando eficiencia, conversión y calidad del lead.
Trabajar de forma coordinada con marketing, ventas, diseño, contenido y operaciones comerciales.
Dar seguimiento al desempeño del funnel digital, desde la campaña hasta el CRM.
Proponer mejoras constantes en audiencias, mensajes, creatividades, landings, formularios y journeys de conversión.
Detectar oportunidades de crecimiento por canal, país, producto, industria y buyer persona.
Presentar resultados, insights y recomendaciones accionables para mejorar el performance de adquisición.
Mantener actualizadas las mejores prácticas de paid media, adquisición digital, automatización, tracking y performance B2B.

description:
About the job

Help Us Turn Visibility Into Growth




Great companies don't grow because they post more content, they grow because the right people notice them.




We're looking for a Marketing & Growth Coordinator who can help expand our reach, strengthen our brand, and generate new business opportunities. This role combines content creation, social media management, outbound marketing, lead generation, and first-touch client engagement. If you enjoy turning ideas into action and helping businesses grow, we'd love to meet you.




What You'll Do

Drive Marketing & Business Growth

Execute marketing initiatives that increase brand visibility and attract new clients
Support pipeline growth through outbound campaigns, targeted outreach, and lead generation
Identify target industries and markets that align with company growth goals
Help generate inbound opportunities through consistent marketing efforts

Build Our Brand

Manage company social media platforms, including LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, and more
Create engaging written, visual, and video content highlighting company updates, hiring success, and client outcomes
Monitor engagement and maintain a consistent online presence

Support Sales & Client Outreach

Launch outbound email and messaging campaigns
Build and maintain prospect lists
Respond to inbound inquiries and conduct first-touch discovery calls with prospective U.S.-based clients
Communicate confidently with business owners and decision-makers

Create Marketing Assets

Develop presentations, one-pagers, branded collateral, and business development materials
Assist with basic video editing, content formatting, and branding initiatives
Help ensure consistent messaging across all marketing channels

Collaborate Across Teams

Work closely with leadership and internal teams to support company growth initiatives
Gather success stories, updates, and content that strengthen our marketing efforts




What You Bring

Experience in marketing, social media management, lead generation, outbound marketing, or business development
Proven success growing audiences, increasing engagement, generating leads, or supporting sales efforts
Ability to showcase measurable marketing results, campaigns, or content performance
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Confidence speaking with U.S.-based business owners and prospects
Experience creating content across multiple platforms
Familiarity with social media management and outbound email marketing
Basic video editing and content formatting skills are a plus
Excellent organization, attention to detail, and ability to manage multiple priorities independently




Why This Role Matters

Every campaign, social post, outreach message, and client conversation creates an opportunity for growth. Your work will directly increase our brand visibility, strengthen client relationships, generate qualified leads, and help drive the company's long-term success.




Why Join Us?

Fully remote opportunity
Join a collaborative and growing team
Make a direct impact on business growth
Build your career with opportunities for long-term development

If you're excited about combining marketing, content creation, outreach, and relationship-building to create measurable business results, we'd love to hear from you.

title: PPC Specialist (Lead Generation, Home Services)
description:
About the job

About company

We are a fast-growing performance agency focused on lead generation for home-services brands. We value ethics, clarity, and measurable outcomes. The team invests in learning and avoids siloed hand-offs so people can own results end to end.




The role

Run PPC for a portfolio of home-services clients and be accountable for outcomes from onboarding through scale. The job blends in-platform execution with tagging, analytics, and client communication. You will work alongside teammates in data and client services rather than sitting in a narrow channel lane.




What you’ll do

• Plan, build, launch, and optimize Google Ads and Microsoft Ads for lead generation

• Join client calls, translate data into decisions, set expectations, and build trust

• Own measurement quality with Google Tag Manager, including data layer checks and debugging

• Use GA4, CRM-based attribution views, Looker Studio, and CallRail to assess performance and recommend changes

• Maintain hygiene, budgets, and a steady test plan; use scripts where helpful

• Collaborate with the existing account team during onboarding and share context with peers




What you’ll bring

• 3-4 or more years in PPC for lead generation, with solid home-services experience

• Strong English for a client-facing role; accents are welcome

• Hands-on strength in Google Ads or Microsoft Ads plus GTM, GA4, Sheets, Looker Studio, and basic CallRail usage

• Comfort reading closed-loop data and making decisions from CRM-sourced revenue

• Clear thinking, attention to detail, and the ability to move between strategy and execution

• Ability to align most working hours to the Central time U.S. day; typical weeks average 40 to 50 hours




Nice to have

• Experience with Google Ads scripts or simple automation

• Zapier or similar workflow tools

• Familiarity with Google Ads API concepts and partnering with data engineers

• Landing page tools such as WordPress, Unbounce, or Leadpages




Interview process

• Initial interview

• Paid audit task

• Follow-up interview to review your work

• Decision




Start date

As soon as possible, with respect for notice periods.




Schedule: Align most of your day to the U.S. Central work window; plan to overlap broadly between 7:00 a.m. and 7:00 p.m. CT

title: Product Sales & Marketing Specialist-Global Service Program Compressors (LATAM)
skills: Identify, qualify, and pursue new client engagements for Long Term Programs (LTP), Operations & Maintenance (O&M), and Service Contracts in the Latin America region.
Develop and implement sales strategies to protect, grow, and diversify relationships with key customers, EPCs, and consultants.
Generate tailored sales proposals and cost models for complex service solutions, coordinating with internal experts to define viable solutions for the compressor portfolio.
Ensure the successful integration of multiple products and services (e.g., Revamps/Mods, Parts, Field Service, Controls) into a cohesive value proposition.
Lead or support customer negotiations from initial contact through to contract closure, ensuring alignment with Siemens Energy’s business interests and revenue goals.
Build and maintain relationships with key stakeholders across customer organizations, influencing their long-term service and maintenance strategies.
Evaluate project risks and opportunities, articulating them to senior management during the bid approval process.
Develop new business models and advanced commercial concepts for service program contracts based on market analysis and competitive intelligence.
Analyze market trends within the Oil & Gas and Industrial Power Generation sectors to inform product strategies and identify cross-divisional opportunities.
Provide market feedback to support the development and implementation of new service products, processes, or operational standards that impact functional results.
Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in Mechanical, Electrical, or Industrial Engineering or equivalent technical studies from an accredited university or relevant experience in lieu of degree.

Extensive years of relevant experience in technical sales, business development, or project management, preferably within the Oil & Gas or Power Generation industries. Those with more experience may be considered for a more senior level position.
Strong sales and commercial acumen with a proven track record in consultative selling and contract negotiation.
Expertise in strategic account management and the ability to develop and implement effective sales strategies.
Technical knowledge of compressor systems and service program design (LTP, O&M) strongly preferred.
Financial acumen in cost modeling and risk analysis, along with strong proposal management and stakeholder management skills.
Fluent in spoken and written Spanish and English is required. Portuguese is a plus. Willingness to travel (approx. 25%).
Career growth and development opportunities; supportive work culture
Company paid Health and wellness benefits
Paid Time Off and paid holidays
Parental leave
Opportunity to work with a global team
Additional rewards based on your country will be further discussed with the hiring team
description:
About the job

A Snapshot of Your Day

In this position, you will play a crucial role in driving the marketing and sales of Compressor Long Term Programs (LTP) and associated services for the Latin America region. As an Expert Professional within the Global Service Operations team, you will focus on offering Long Term Service Programs, Operations and Maintenance, on the new and installed fleet. Your responsibilities will encompass three core areas: Strategic Sales & Business Development for acquiring high-value Long Term Service Program accounts, Technical Solution & Proposal Management for creating tailored service program offerings, and Market & Product Strategy to ensure our service programs remain competitive and innovative. Your extensive experience in technical sales within the Oil & Gas or Industrial Power Generation markets will be essential for success in this role.

How You’ll Make An Impact


Identify, qualify, and pursue new client engagements for Long Term Programs (LTP), Operations & Maintenance (O&M), and Service Contracts in the Latin America region.
Develop and implement sales strategies to protect, grow, and diversify relationships with key customers, EPCs, and consultants.
Generate tailored sales proposals and cost models for complex service solutions, coordinating with internal experts to define viable solutions for the compressor portfolio.
Ensure the successful integration of multiple products and services (e.g., Revamps/Mods, Parts, Field Service, Controls) into a cohesive value proposition.
Lead or support customer negotiations from initial contact through to contract closure, ensuring alignment with Siemens Energy’s business interests and revenue goals.
Build and maintain relationships with key stakeholders across customer organizations, influencing their long-term service and maintenance strategies.
Evaluate project risks and opportunities, articulating them to senior management during the bid approval process.
Develop new business models and advanced commercial concepts for service program contracts based on market analysis and competitive intelligence.
Analyze market trends within the Oil & Gas and Industrial Power Generation sectors to inform product strategies and identify cross-divisional opportunities.
Provide market feedback to support the development and implementation of new service products, processes, or operational standards that impact functional results.



What You Bring


Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in Mechanical, Electrical, or Industrial Engineering or equivalent technical studies from an accredited university or relevant experience in lieu of degree.
Extensive years of relevant experience in technical sales, business development, or project management, preferably within the Oil & Gas or Power Generation industries. Those with more experience may be considered for a more senior level position.
Strong sales and commercial acumen with a proven track record in consultative selling and contract negotiation.
Expertise in strategic account management and the ability to develop and implement effective sales strategies.
Technical knowledge of compressor systems and service program design (LTP, O&M) strongly preferred.
Financial acumen in cost modeling and risk analysis, along with strong proposal management and stakeholder management skills.
Fluent in spoken and written Spanish and English is required. Portuguese is a plus. Willingness to travel (approx. 25%).



About The Team

Our Transformation of Industry division is decarbonizing the industrial sector. Increasing electrification and efficiency are key and demand for green H2 and derivative fuels will rise. We enable decarbonization of the industrial sector and the transition to sustainable processes, building on a strong industrial customer base, a global network, diverse technologies, and integrated execution capabilities. Candidates want to learn about the divisions they will be joining–the structure, how it works together, and the role it plays in driving Siemens Energy’s mission forward.

Who is Siemens Energy?

At Siemens Energy, we are more than just an energy technology company. We meet the growing energy demand across 90+ countries while ensuring our climate is protected. With 100,000 dedicated employees, we not only generate electricity for over 16% of the global community, but we’re also using our technology to help protect people and the environment.

Our global team is committed to making sustainable, reliable, and affordable energy a reality by pushing the boundaries of what is possible. We uphold a 150-year legacy of innovation that encourages our search for people who will support our focus on decarbonization, new technologies, and energy transformation.

Find out how you can make a difference at Siemens Energy: https://www.siemens-energy.com/employeevideo

Rewards/Benefits


Career growth and development opportunities; supportive work culture
Company paid Health and wellness benefits
Paid Time Off and paid holidays
Parental leave
Opportunity to work with a global team
Additional rewards based on your country will be further discussed with the hiring team

title: [Annotations] Operations Associate
skills: Build and drive some of our most critical operational processes
Own the day-to-day delivery of customer commitments
Create an effective feedback loop between the front line, product, strategy, and customers
Collaborate with stakeholders to improve processes for new and existing customers
Advanced English skills
Industry experience (+2) in an operational role and/or a top-tier consulting firm
An undergraduate degree with an analytics heavy major (e.g., Engineering or Economics) and/or a graduate degree in Engineering, Economics, or Business
An action-oriented mindset that balances creative problem solving with the scrappiness to ultimately deliver results
Analytical, planning, and process improvement capability
Experience with reading SQL, or have demonstrated analytical skills
description:
About the job

As a member of our operations team, you will be accountable for driving revenue by ensuring that Scale AI meets customer commitments in a timely manner while maintaining the highest quality standards. You will manage our supply operation funnel by building and running solutions, tools, and processes by working with a cross-functional team including Customer Operations, Product Operations, Product Managers, and many others.

You will be solving problems no one has solved before, and you will need to be relentless in driving stellar results, running pilots, tests, and experiments. You’ll come up with creative solutions to bottlenecks. The blend of operations and ownership of our most important outcomes make this a unique and exciting role at the heart of Scale’s daily operations.The ideal candidate is scrappy, analytical, empathetic, outcome focused, and above all someone who drives and inspires results.

You will:


Build and drive some of our most critical operational processes
Own the day-to-day delivery of customer commitments
Create an effective feedback loop between the front line, product, strategy, and customers
Collaborate with stakeholders to improve processes for new and existing customers 


Ideally you'd have:


Advanced English skills
Industry experience (+2) in an operational role and/or a top-tier consulting firm
An undergraduate degree with an analytics heavy major (e.g., Engineering or Economics) and/or a graduate degree in Engineering, Economics, or Business
An action-oriented mindset that balances creative problem solving with the scrappiness to ultimately deliver results
Analytical, planning, and process improvement capability
Experience with reading SQL, or have demonstrated analytical skills


PLEASE NOTE: Our policy requires a 90-day waiting period before reconsidering candidates for the same role. This allows us to ensure a fair and thorough evaluation of all applicants.

About Us:

At Scale, our mission is to develop reliable AI systems for the world's most important decisions. Our products provide the high-quality data and full-stack technologies that power the world's leading models, and help enterprises and governments build, deploy, and oversee AI applications that deliver real impact. We work closely with industry leaders like Meta, Ernst & Young, Mayo Clinic, Time Inc., the Government of Qatar, and U.S. government agencies including the Army and Air Force. We are expanding our team to accelerate the development of AI applications.

We believe that everyone should be able to bring their whole selves to work, which is why we are proud to be an inclusive and equal opportunity workplace. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability status, gender identity or Veteran status. 

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with physical and mental disabilities. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation in the application or recruiting process due to a disability, please contact us at accommodations@scale.com. Please see the United States Department of Labor's Know Your Rights poster for additional information.

We comply with the United States Department of Labor's Pay Transparency provision. 

PLEASE NOTE: We collect, retain and use personal data for our professional business purposes, including notifying you of job opportunities that may be of interest and sharing with our affiliates. We limit the personal data we collect to that which we believe is appropriate and necessary to manage applicants’ needs, provide our services, and comply with applicable laws. Any information we collect in connection with your application will be treated in accordance with our internal policies and programs designed to protect personal data. Please see our privacy policy for additional information.

description:
About the job

Caylent is a cloud native services company that helps organizations bring the best out of their people and technology using Amazon Web Services (AWS). We provide a full-range of AWS services including workload migrations and modernization, cloud native application development, DevOps, data engineering, security and compliance, and everything in between.

At Caylent, our people always come first. We are a global company and operate fully remote with employees in Canada, the United States, and Latin America. We celebrate the culture of each of our team members and foster a community of technological curiosity. Come talk to us to learn more about what it means to be a Caylien!

The Mission

We're seeking a talented Product Manager for our growing Professional Services team. The ideal candidate has substantial experience in product management within an application development and engineering services company. Your mission is to drive product success by designing customer-centric product roadmaps, interfacing with UI/UX teams, and managing the ongoing backlog of work that flows to engineering teams. Collaborating closely with our customers, you'll ensure they meet their goals, bridging the gap between business needs and technical execution.

Your Assignment

Support Customer/User Research and Journey Design

Work closely with customers to understand their needs, pain points, and goals. Conduct user research and facilitate journey design workshops to map out the customer journey. Use insights gathered to inform product decisions and ensure alignment with customer expectations.

Design Product Roadmaps

Create and maintain comprehensive product roadmaps that align with customer business goals and strategic priorities. Set clear product objectives and metrics, and ensure that roadmaps are regularly updated to reflect evolving customer needs and market trends.

Ongoing Product Management

Manage the product lifecycle from ideation to launch and beyond. Interface with UX teams to ensure that product designs meet user needs and provide a seamless experience. Collaborate with engineering teams to manage the backlog, prioritize features, and ensure timely delivery. Facilitate effective communication between UX and engineering teams to ensure alignment and address any roadblocks promptly.

KPIs:


Utilization
Influenced Pipeline and growth of Customers
CSAT scores within Product Engagements


Your Qualifications


5+ years of product management experience within application development and engineering services
Experience with product roadmap tools and software, running product experiments, and product launches
Experience with UX/UI concepts and technologies
Experience delivering large, complex consulting engagements for enterprise clients
Expertise in product lifecycle management and Agile methodologies
Excellent communication skills, especially with executive audiences
Startup environment adaptability and cross-functional teamwork
Curiosity and eagerness to learn new technologies
Willingness to travel for project kick-offs and key client milestones


Preferred Qualifications


Certifications in AWS, Agile, or related areas
Knowledge of Security Best Practices, Cloud Economics, Serverless technologies, container-based architecture, software development frameworks, and cloud cost optimization
Experience in AWS consulting with Fortune 2000 clients


Benefits


100% remote work
Medical Insurance for you and eligible dependents
Generous holidays and flexible PTO
Competitive phantom equity
Paid for exams and certifications
Peer bonus awards
State of the art laptop and tools
Equipment & Office Stipend
Individual professional development plan
Annual stipend for Learning and Development
Work with an amazing worldwide team and in an incredible corporate culture


This role may require up to 25% travel, depending on business needs.

NOTE: We’re unable to provide visa sponsorship now or at any time in the future.

At Caylent, we are committed to fair, transparent, and inclusive hiring practices. As part of our recruitment process, we may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools or automated systems to assist with the screening and evaluation of applications to help match candidate qualifications with job requirements.

These tools are designed to support — not replace — human decision-making. Final hiring decisions are always made by our trained recruitment professionals.

If an AI or automated tool is used during your application process, it will only be in accordance with applicable laws and regulations, and your information will be handled in a secure and confidential manner.

If you have any questions, please contact talent@caylent.com

Caylent is a place where everyone belongs. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. Our approach helps us to build a winning team that represents a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and abilities. So, regardless of how your diversity expresses itself, you can find a home here at Caylent.

We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind based on race, color, religion, national origin, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, veteran status, genetic information, disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics. If you would like to request an accommodation due to a disability, please contact us at hr@caylent.com.

description:
About the job

Descripción Empresa

El Puerto de Liverpool is the leading department store chain in Mexico. With over 170 years in the market, we are dedicated to simplifying product selection and providing the best service to Mexican families, consistently exceeding expectations in quality, fashion, value, and customer care within a world-class environment.

We operate five strategic business units: Liverpool Stores, Suburbia Stores, Galerías Shopping Centers, Boutiques, and Financial Services, forming a solid corporation that offers our collaborators financial stability and the pride of belonging to a 100% Mexican Company.

Our Mission: To serve the customer everywhere, every day, for a lifetime.

Our Vision: To be the best option in service, assortment, and value.

Our Values: Our work is guided by teamwork, productivity, innovation, and integrity.

Misión Del Cargo

At El Puerto de Liverpool, we are looking for "the best of the best" to join our Senior Commercial Development Program.

Funciones Del Cargo

This is a high-exposure strategic position designed for professionals who aim to lead the retail industry. As part of our Senior Commercial Development Program, you will undergo a 6-month rotation across our core business units (Store Operations, Stockline, and Hardline) to master the "Liverpool Business Model."

Your mission is to bridge the gap between corporate strategy and point-of-sale execution, identifying growth opportunities and preparing to take a permanent leadership role within our Commercial Direction.

Key Responsibilities


 Business Intelligence: Analyze sales data, market trends, and inventory turnover to provide actionable insights for various categories (Fashion, Home, Multimedia, etc.).
 Strategic Rotation: Collaborate with Sub-Directors and senior buyers to understand the end-to-end commercial cycle, from global sourcing to local sales floor performance.
 Operational Excellence: Spent time in-store to understand the customer journey and ensure that corporate strategies translate into a premium shopping experience.
 Stakeholder Management: Work closely with cross-functional teams (Logistics, Marketing, and Finance) to align commercial goals with company-wide KPIs.


The Ideal Candidate


 Commercial Expertise: A solid background in commercial areas (Sales, Category Management, or Business Development) with a proven track record of driving results.
 Passion for Procurement: A genuine interest in developing a career
 Business Visionary: You don’t just "buy" products; you understand the market, the competition, and the consumer.
 Data-Driven: Advanced proficiency in Excel and mathematical reasoning to handle complex commercial budgets.
 Effective Communicator: 100% English proficiency is required to navigate global trends and internal reporting.
 Leadership Potential: A proactive mindset with the ability to learn fast and adapt to different business environments.


Requisitos


solid background in commercial areas
Advanced Business English
Strategic Financial Mindset
Proficient in Analytical Tools


Beneficios

Condiciones oferta:

Benefits & Perks


 Comprehensive Health Insurance
 Continuous Training & Development Courses
 Employee Store Discounts
 Grocery Vouchers
 Flexible Working Hours
 Birthday Off
 Additional Vacation Days

 Postular a la oferta en el link

title: Analista Comercial de Cuentas - Marketplace
skills: Vibran energía emprendedora: se mueven por la curiosidad, nunca se rinden y se enfocan en superar sus propios límites.
Dan el máximo porque les gusta trabajar con compromiso y dedicación.
Viven los cambios como oportunidades y aprenden de sus errores.
La excelencia y la ejecución son claves en su forma de hacer las cosas.
Promueven el buen clima, aportan alegría y diversión.
Saben cómo construir con otras personas y disfrutan trabajando en equipo.

Definir la visión y estrategia de negocio para las marcas del segmento de Supermercado. Manejar el P&L y desarrollar planes para lograr los objetivos de rentabilidad y crecimiento esperados así como ser responsable del desempeño y planeación de inventarios, asegurando una correcta disponibilidad y rotación de inventarios.

Establecer sinergias y relaciones estrechas con las marcas que permitan un crecimiento sostenible y sustentable. Es el encargado de liderar las negociaciones para asegurar los objetivos de rentabilidad y crecimiento.

Establecer planes y asegurar una selección óptima, competitiva y completa que nos permita asegurar una óptima experiencia de compra para el cliente, contando con una alta calidad en publicaciones (imágenes y contenido) y selección.

Generación de análisis y documentos que permitan un correcto seguimiento a KPIs de manera oportuna y eficiente y colaborar con equipos internos para generar herramientas y procesos automáticos que permitan flujos de trabajo más eficientes.
Planeación de ciclos de vida de producto que permitan una correcta ejecución de cambios de lineal dentro de las marcas a cargo.
Ser profesional de las carreras de Cs Economicas o Ingenieria.
Contar con experiencia de 2 años en roles de Compras, Ventas, Mkt o similares, llevando la gestión de cuentas, desarrollando negociaciones y manejando el P&L.
Es deseable contar con manejo de bases de datos como SQL, Looker, Data Studio o Power BI.
Ser parte de una compañía con espíritu emprendedor en la que nos encanta pensar en grande y a largo plazo.
Ser protagonista de tu desarrollo en un ambiente de oportunidades, aprendizaje, crecimiento, expansión y proyectos desafiantes.
Compartir y aprender en equipo junto a grandes profesionales y especialistas.
Un excelente clima de trabajo, con todo lo necesario para que vivas una gran experiencia. :)
description:
About the job

En Mercado Libre estamos democratizando el comercio, el dinero y los pagos en América Latina.




Nuestro Marketplace es el lugar donde las personas que compran y las que venden encuentran la más amplia oferta de productos y servicios disponible al precio más competitivo del mercado. Constantemente buscamos mejorar nuestros servicios comerciales, de producto y marketing garantizando una experiencia única para quienes ingresan a nuestro sitio. ¿Te gustaría revolucionar el e-commerce?




Tenemos un desafío para quienes:

Vibran energía emprendedora: se mueven por la curiosidad, nunca se rinden y se enfocan en superar sus propios límites.
Dan el máximo porque les gusta trabajar con compromiso y dedicación.
Viven los cambios como oportunidades y aprenden de sus errores.
La excelencia y la ejecución son claves en su forma de hacer las cosas.
Promueven el buen clima, aportan alegría y diversión.
Saben cómo construir con otras personas y disfrutan trabajando en equipo.




Imaginate emprendiendo proyectos desafiantes, dinámicos e innovadores y siendo responsable de:

Definir la visión y estrategia de negocio para las marcas del segmento de Supermercado. Manejar el P&L y desarrollar planes para lograr los objetivos de rentabilidad y crecimiento esperados así como ser responsable del desempeño y planeación de inventarios, asegurando una correcta disponibilidad y rotación de inventarios.
Establecer sinergias y relaciones estrechas con las marcas que permitan un crecimiento sostenible y sustentable. Es el encargado de liderar las negociaciones para asegurar los objetivos de rentabilidad y crecimiento.
Establecer planes y asegurar una selección óptima, competitiva y completa que nos permita asegurar una óptima experiencia de compra para el cliente, contando con una alta calidad en publicaciones (imágenes y contenido) y selección.
Generación de análisis y documentos que permitan un correcto seguimiento a KPIs de manera oportuna y eficiente y colaborar con equipos internos para generar herramientas y procesos automáticos que permitan flujos de trabajo más eficientes.
Planeación de ciclos de vida de producto que permitan una correcta ejecución de cambios de lineal dentro de las marcas a cargo.




Requisitos:

Ser profesional de las carreras de Cs Economicas o Ingenieria.
Contar con experiencia de 2 años en roles de Compras, Ventas, Mkt o similares, llevando la gestión de cuentas, desarrollando negociaciones y manejando el P&L.
Es deseable contar con manejo de bases de datos como SQL, Looker, Data Studio o Power BI.




Te proponemos:

Ser parte de una compañía con espíritu emprendedor en la que nos encanta pensar en grande y a largo plazo.
Ser protagonista de tu desarrollo en un ambiente de oportunidades, aprendizaje, crecimiento, expansión y proyectos desafiantes.
Compartir y aprender en equipo junto a grandes profesionales y especialistas.
Un excelente clima de trabajo, con todo lo necesario para que vivas una gran experiencia. :)

title: Ads Sales Manager
skills: Developing and implementing innovative advertising strategies, from ideation to campaign execution, ensuring measurable business impact.
Collaborating with internal teams (Product, Marketing, Data, Operations) to optimize ad performance and deliver an exceptional customer experience.
Building and strengthening relationships with external partners (global brands, agencies, new advertisers), negotiating deals that drive the adoption of our advertising solutions.
Analyzing performance metrics and leveraging data insights to identify continuous improvements and maximize campaign outcomes.
Influencing product development by proposing new ad formats and contributing to the long-term vision of DiDi Ads.
Supporting and guiding your team and stakeholders, fostering a culture of win-win collaboration, integrity, and ongoing growth.
Delivering impactful presentations to executives and clients, showcasing the value and benefits of our advertising solutions
5 years of experience in digital advertising strategy, ideally within large-scale platforms or fast-paced environments.
A proven track record in negotiation, account management, and consultative sales for enterprise-level clients, backed by strong communication skills.
A bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, Engineering, or a related field.
Advanced data analysis capabilities to make insight-driven decisions.
An agile and collaborative mindset, aligned with our values of creating value, data-based thinking, win-win collaboration, integrity, growth, and diversity.
Professional fluency in English, allowing you to confidently present ideas and influence global teams.
description:
About the job

About The Company

DiDi Global Inc. is the world’s leading mobility technology platform. It offers a wide range of app-based services across markets including Asia-Pacific, Latin America and Africa, including ride hailing, taxi hailing, chauffeur, hitch and other forms of shared mobility as well as auto solutions, food delivery, intra-city freight, and financial services.

DiDi provides car owners, drivers, and delivery partners with flexible work and income opportunities. It is committed to collaborating with policymakers, the taxi industry, the automobile industry and the communities to solve the world’s transportation, environmental and employment challenges through the use of AI technology and localized smart transportation innovations. DiDi strives to create better life experiences and greater social value, by building a safe, inclusive and sustainable transportation and local services ecosystem for cities of the future.

For more information, please visit: www.didiglobal.com/news

About The Team/role

In this role, you’ll be at the forefront of designing and executing high-impact advertising strategies, working cross-functionally (Product, Marketing, Operations) and partnering with top-tier clients.

We’re looking for a strategic, data-driven, and resourceful trailblazer who thrives on challenges and is eager to elevate our Ads platform to the next level.

In this role, you'll be...


Developing and implementing innovative advertising strategies, from ideation to campaign execution, ensuring measurable business impact.
Collaborating with internal teams (Product, Marketing, Data, Operations) to optimize ad performance and deliver an exceptional customer experience.
Building and strengthening relationships with external partners (global brands, agencies, new advertisers), negotiating deals that drive the adoption of our advertising solutions.
Analyzing performance metrics and leveraging data insights to identify continuous improvements and maximize campaign outcomes.
Influencing product development by proposing new ad formats and contributing to the long-term vision of DiDi Ads.
Supporting and guiding your team and stakeholders, fostering a culture of win-win collaboration, integrity, and ongoing growth.
Delivering impactful presentations to executives and clients, showcasing the value and benefits of our advertising solutions



We're eager to be in touch because you have...


5 years of experience in digital advertising strategy, ideally within large-scale platforms or fast-paced environments.
A proven track record in negotiation, account management, and consultative sales for enterprise-level clients, backed by strong communication skills.
A bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, Engineering, or a related field.
Advanced data analysis capabilities to make insight-driven decisions.
An agile and collaborative mindset, aligned with our values of creating value, data-based thinking, win-win collaboration, integrity, growth, and diversity.
Professional fluency in English, allowing you to confidently present ideas and influence global teams.



You'll love working at DiDi because...

We create user value

We strive to always create valuable experiences for our users in everything we do. Our focus is to always innovate new experiences that are safe, pleasant and efficient.

We are data-driven

We are strong believers in making informed decisions, that’s why we are data-driven. We can better navigate the business landscape strategically by analyzing valuable metrics.

Win-win Collaboration

Success is a team sport. When we work to help our partners and colleagues win, we win, too. While keeping everyone's best interest at heart, we communicate with candor and execute with excellence in all we do.

We believe in integrity

Integrity is at the very core of our business. We are people who always want to do the right thing. Our intentions are sincere, we speak our minds and listen to each other.

Growth

We always strive to do better. That means venturing beyond our comfort zones, learning from our mistakes, and helping each other grow.

Diversity and Inclusion

Diversity is one of our biggest strengths. Our differences are what make us distinct. We respect each other and believe in equal opportunities for all.

We are committed to building inclusive and diverse teams

At DiDi, we believe that our differences are our biggest source of strength. That’s why we are committed to promoting equal opportunities to all candidates and employees as an Equal Opportunity Employer.

Employment and advancement decisions at DiDi are always made based on the needs of the position and the qualifications of the candidate. We do not discriminate against any employee or applicant based on their gender, age, sexual orientation, nationality, marital status, pregnancy/maternity, disability, race, religion and beliefs, or any other status protected by applicable laws wherever we operate.

We are committed to building inclusive and diverse teams, and a workplace that is free from discrimination and harassment, because that’s how we create better products and services, make better decisions and better serve the communities we’re a part of.

description:
About the job

Job Title: Salesforce Developer (Remote)
Work Mode: Fully Remote
Schedule: Flexible (up to 40 hours per week, based on project availability)




Role Overview:

We are looking for software engineers across Latin America to join a paid remote AI training program. In this role, you will review and evaluate AI-generated code, rate AI responses for accuracy and quality, and write coding challenges to help improve AI model performance. This is a flexible, remote opportunity well-suited for software engineers seeking project-based, asynchronous work. No prior AI experience required.




Key Responsibilities

Review and evaluate code written by AI models for accuracy, efficiency, and best practices
Rate AI responses across key dimensions including correctness, instruction-following, and quality
Write original computer science questions and coding challenges to test AI capabilities
Identify bugs, errors, and inconsistencies in AI-generated code using structured guidelines
Provide clear, constructive, and structured written feedback to improve AI responses
Follow project guidelines, quality standards, and confidentiality requirements




Qualifications

Software engineering experience — junior, mid-level, or senior welcome
Proficiency in one or more of the following: Python, Java, JavaScript, TypeScript, C++, Swift, or Rust
Strong attention to detail, analytical thinking, and problem-solving skills
Background in full stack, backend, frontend, mobile, or any engineering discipline




Eligibility Requirements

Candidates must be legally authorised to work in one of the following locations:
Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Belize, Chile, Colombia, Costa Rica, Dominican Republic, Dominica, Ecuador, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Mexico, Nicaragua, Panama, Paraguay, Peru, Puerto Rico, Trinidad and Tobago, Uruguay, Venezuela




Compensation

Earn up to $34 USD/hr — core project rate with additional performance incentives averaging 7.5% on top
Payments issued weekly via supported payout platforms (e.g., PayPal or AirTM)




Work Arrangement

Fully remote and asynchronous
Flexible workload depending on project availability
Free access to leading AI tools worth $200+/month included




Equal Opportunity Statement Selection decisions are based solely on skills, qualifications, and project requirements. We are committed to inclusive and fair engagement practices and consider all qualified applicants without regard to legally protected characteristics.




Ready to put your coding skills to work on AI? Apply now — interviews begin within days of application.

title: Creative Technologist
skills: Architect, evaluate, and refine AI-powered production workflows for creative adaptation and digital display.

Oversee the development and adaptation of HTML5 ads and creative materials, using tools such as GSAP and Google Web Designer. Ensure all assets are resized, optimised, and delivered on time to meet the needs of the campaign.
Manage the process of creating and optimising dynamic creative content for large-scale campaigns, using platforms like Flashtalking, Celtra, and Google Creative Studio.

Strategically map the production lifecycle from initial brief to final delivery, pinpointing opportunities to enhance operational velocity, consistency, and creative scalability through advanced automation, structured data inputs, and superior tooling.
Work closely with design, development, and media teams to ensure a seamless production process, providing clear guidance and feedback to ensure the highest quality outputs.
Ensure all deliverables meet high standards of quality and functionality.
Stay updated with industry trends, emerging technologies, and new platforms relevant to digital ad production and creative technology.
Manage the preparation and localization of assets, ensuring that creative assets are appropriately adapted for different markets and channels
5+ years of experience in digital production, creative technology, or similar roles, with a strong focus on digital ad development and optimization.

Demonstrable curiosity and applied knowledge of generative AI within creative ecosystems, specifically focusing on agentic methodologies, nodal architectures, and the deployment of automated, interconnected production tooling.
Solid understanding of DCO (Dynamic Creative Optimization), including working with dynamic feeds, creative updates, and tools like Google Creative Studio and Ads Creative Studio.
Proficiency in HTML5, CSS, and JavaScript, with experience in hand-coding interactive and animated display ads.
Strong skills in Adobe Suite (After Effects, Photoshop, Illustrator), Figma, and 3D software (Blender is a bonus).
Experience with AI-powered platforms
Excellent project management skills with experience balancing multiple projects and deadlines.
Advanced English
description:
About the job

At Jellyfish we believe that our individual differences are our greatest assets. We are passionate about empowering every employee to reach their full potential and contribute their unique perspectives to our collaborative environment. We are an equal opportunity employer and firmly believe that a diverse and inclusive workforce drives innovation and leads to better solutions. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other personal characteristics that make them who they are.

Jellyfish is a global digital marketing agency; a unique fusion of tech enthusiasts, creative minds, and media and data experts all united to empower our clients along their digital journey. Our commitment to embracing diverse perspectives fuels our innovation and strategies that challenge the status quo, reinvent media activation, and craft influential stories for our global clients and their customers. Join us in shaping a future where business growth and personal fulfilment go hand in hand.

Job Description

We are seeking a systems-oriented creative production expert who excels at building and troubleshooting HTML5 display ads but is even more passionate about revolutionizing creative workflows.

In the role of Creative Technologist, you will spearhead the transformation of HTML5 display production by implementing AI-enhanced workflows and automated, scalable systems. Collaborating with creative, strategy, media, and development teams, you will convert campaign goals into efficient production frameworks. Your influence will span the entire lifecycle, defining how briefs, prompts, feeds, and variants integrate with rigorous QA and approval steps.

Responsibilities


Architect, evaluate, and refine AI-powered production workflows for creative adaptation and digital display.
Oversee the development and adaptation of HTML5 ads and creative materials, using tools such as GSAP and Google Web Designer. Ensure all assets are resized, optimised, and delivered on time to meet the needs of the campaign.
Manage the process of creating and optimising dynamic creative content for large-scale campaigns, using platforms like Flashtalking, Celtra, and Google Creative Studio.
Strategically map the production lifecycle from initial brief to final delivery, pinpointing opportunities to enhance operational velocity, consistency, and creative scalability through advanced automation, structured data inputs, and superior tooling.
Work closely with design, development, and media teams to ensure a seamless production process, providing clear guidance and feedback to ensure the highest quality outputs.
Ensure all deliverables meet high standards of quality and functionality.
Stay updated with industry trends, emerging technologies, and new platforms relevant to digital ad production and creative technology.
Manage the preparation and localization of assets, ensuring that creative assets are appropriately adapted for different markets and channels


Qualifications


5+ years of experience in digital production, creative technology, or similar roles, with a strong focus on digital ad development and optimization.
Demonstrable curiosity and applied knowledge of generative AI within creative ecosystems, specifically focusing on agentic methodologies, nodal architectures, and the deployment of automated, interconnected production tooling.
Solid understanding of DCO (Dynamic Creative Optimization), including working with dynamic feeds, creative updates, and tools like Google Creative Studio and Ads Creative Studio.
Proficiency in HTML5, CSS, and JavaScript, with experience in hand-coding interactive and animated display ads.
Strong skills in Adobe Suite (After Effects, Photoshop, Illustrator), Figma, and 3D software (Blender is a bonus).
Experience with AI-powered platforms
Excellent project management skills with experience balancing multiple projects and deadlines.
Advanced English


Note: We emphasise skills, expertise and behavioural attributes over years of experience and traditional degrees. If you want to join our collaborative team, we invite you to apply today with your resume in English.

Additional Information

Join Jellyfish and experience a workplace where we prioritise your growth, celebrate your contributions, and empower you to tailor your work environment to suit your needs.

💰 Savings fund: You'll save 5% of your monthly income and Jellyfish will contribute the same amount you save to your savings fund.

💫 Custom Work Environment: Work remotely for up to 60% of your days and shape your day between 8am. and 6:30pm with flexible working hours.

🏥 Health Insurance: We provide a private health insurance policy that includes visual and dental plan coverage.

Unfortunately, there has been an increase in fake recruiters impersonating Jellyfish and unlawfully using our brand name. If you are unsure if an email with a job offer you have received is genuinely from Jellyfish, or if you suspect any fraudulent activity, please report it to talentacquisition@jellyfish.com.

title: Search Senior Executive, GOC
skills: Be Extraordinary by Leading Collectively to Inspire transformational Creativity.
Create an Open environment by Balancing People and Client Experiences by Cultivating Trust.
Lead Optimistically by Championing Growth and Development to Mobilize the Enterprise.
Develop strong relationships with counterparts in WPP Media’s regional teams (Europe, United States and Asia).
Support the implementation of digital campaigns and ads according to guidelines with platforms such as Google Ads, Bing Ads, Amazon Search, Search Ads 360, etc.
Ensure campaigns comply with WPP Media’s global policies and guidelines.
Monitor campaigns daily on each of the platforms and ensure accuracy of data across platforms for campaign reporting.
Generate daily campaign reports for reporting to regional Search teams.
Bachelor’s degree.
Advanced English level (B2–C2).
Have 1.5-2 years of experience implementing paid Search campaigns on platforms such as Google Ads, Bing Ads, Amazon Search, etc.
Advanced Excel skills.
description:
About the job

About WPP Media

WPP is the trusted growth partner for the world’s leading brands. With exceptional talent, trusted data and intelligence, and world-class partnerships – all united by our pioneering agentic marketing platform, WPP Open – we help clients navigate change, capture opportunity, and deliver transformational growth. 

WPP Media is WPP's AI-driven media operating unit, bringing together media, data, and partnerships to deliver creative personalisation at scale. Connected through WPP Open and powered by Open Intelligence, clients see exactly where, how, and why their media investment is working.

For more information, visit wppmedia.com.

Senior Executive Search, GOC

About WPP Media

WPP is the creative transformation company. We use the power of creativity to build better futures for our people, planet, clients and communities. For more information, visit wpp.com.

WPP Media is WPP’s global media collective. In a world where media is everywhere and in everything, we bring the best platform, people, and partners together to create limitless opportunities for growth. For more information, visit wppmedia.com

At WPP Media, We Believe In The Power Of Our Culture And Our People. It’s What Elevates Us To Deliver Exceptional Experiences For Both Our Clients And Each Other. In This Role It Will Be Critical To Embrace WPP & WPP Media’s Shared Core Values:


Be Extraordinary by Leading Collectively to Inspire transformational Creativity. 
Create an Open environment by Balancing People and Client Experiences by Cultivating Trust. 
Lead Optimistically by Championing Growth and Development to Mobilize the Enterprise. 


Role Summary And Impact

As part of the Global Operations Centre (GOC), you will work closely with teams across the United States, Europe, and Asia to deliver high-impact solutions in a collaborative, multicultural environment. We are looking for a Senior Executive Search who will:


Develop strong relationships with counterparts in WPP Media’s regional teams (Europe, United States and Asia). 
Support the implementation of digital campaigns and ads according to guidelines with platforms such as Google Ads, Bing Ads, Amazon Search, Search Ads 360, etc. 
Ensure campaigns comply with WPP Media’s global policies and guidelines. 
Monitor campaigns daily on each of the platforms and ensure accuracy of data across platforms for campaign reporting. 
Generate daily campaign reports for reporting to regional Search teams. 


Skills And Experience

If this opportunity looks right for you, we are looking for candidates who have:


Bachelor’s degree. 
Advanced English level (B2–C2). 
Have 1.5-2 years of experience implementing paid Search campaigns on platforms such as Google Ads, Bing Ads, Amazon Search, etc. 
Advanced Excel skills. 


Life At WPP Media & Benefits

Our passion for shaping the next era of media includes investing in our employees to help them do their best work, and we’re just as committed to employee growth as we are to responsible media investment. WPP Media employees can tap into the global WPP Media & WPP networks to pursue their passions, grow their networks, and learn at the cutting edge of marketing and advertising. We have a variety of employee resource groups and host frequent in-office events showcasing team wins, sharing thought leadership, and celebrating holidays and milestone events. Our benefits include access to internal learning platforms, certifications, and training sessions, life insurance, wellness programs, significant paid time off, preferential partner discounts, employee mental health awareness day, and more. 

WPP Media is an equal opportunity employer and considers applicants for all positions without discrimination or regard to particular characteristics. We are committed to fostering a culture of respect in which everyone feels they belong and has the same opportunities to progress in their careers.

We believe the best work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. If you require accommodations or flexibility, please discuss this with the hiring team during the interview process.

For Mexico:

This position is available in a Remote or Hybrid Work Modality. The candidate must perform their work from any location within Mexico. Only Mexican nationals residing in the country or foreign nationals with a valid work permit may apply. Remote work from abroad is not permitted under any circumstances.

For Colombia:

This position is in a Remote or Hybrid Work Modality and must be performed within Colombian territory. Only candidates residing in Colombia with a valid work permit may apply. Remote work contracts do not allow employment or relocation abroad.

Please read our Privacy Notice (https://www.wppmedia.com/pages/privacy-policy ) for more information on how we process the information you provide.

While we appreciate all applications received, only those candidates selected for an interview will be contacted. 

Please read our Privacy Notice for more information on how we process the information you provide.

description:
About the job

Straive is a global leader in enterprise-grade data analytics and AI solutions, committed to empowering businesses across various industries with cutting-edge technology and expert insights. Backed by EQT, a top private equity firm, we are uniquely positioned to drive innovation through significant investments and an entrepreneurial spirit.




Our core focus is on delivering advanced Data Analytics & AI Solutions. By combining sophisticated technology with subject matter expertise, we deliver material impact on our clients' topline and streamline their operations. We specialize in providing tailored solutions across financial services, CPG, legal, pharma, life sciences, retail and logistics, helping them build robust data analytics and AI capabilities.




With a client base spanning 30 countries, Straive's strategically located teams operate from eight countries and is headquartered in Singapore. This global presence enables us to offer localized expertise with a worldwide perspective.




Join Straive to be part of a dynamic team at the forefront of data analytics and AI innovation. Here, you'll have the opportunity to contribute to transformative projects, supported by significant investments and an entrepreneurial drive fueled by our partnership with EQT.




Website: https://www.straive.com/




Job Title: L1 Search Evaluator

Location: Masaryk, Polanco, Mexico City, Mexico

Job Type: FTE




Role Overview: L1 Search Evaluators are the frontline specialists responsible for assessing search quality and investigating user feedback to improve Client’s algorithms and search products.

Key Responsibilities:

Search Quality Assessment: Review and categorize "bad cases" to help search products and algorithms improve.
User Feedback Investigation: Analyze user search requirements through data and direct feedback.
Optimization & Risk: Provide suggestions for search result improvements and identify potential trust and safety risks.
Policy Application: Read and apply 20-page long policies to daily evaluation tasks.




Required Qualifications:

Experience: 1+ years of experience in conducting evaluations or data labeling.
Language: High English Proficiency (Reading, Writing, and Speaking).
Technical Skills: Proven ability to navigate, troubleshoot, and maximize the utility of internal platforms.
Cultural Knowledge: In-depth understanding of pop culture and Internet usage habits.
Commitment: Ability to work a 40-hour weekly schedule with a 1-hour turnaround SLA.




Preferred Qualifications:

Experience in search-related or Internet-related business.
Background in content moderation or user feedback operations.
Heavy social media user.




This job description is not intended to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of all responsibilities, duties, or activities that are required. Responsibilities, duties, and/or activities may change, or new ones may be added at any time with or without notice.




If you are a motivated professional with a passion for delivering impactful solutions, we’d love to hear from you. Apply today to be part of a dynamic and forward-thinking team at Straive.




“Straive is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Our policy is clear: there shall be no discrimination based on age, disability, sex, race, religion or belief, gender reassignment, marriage/civil partnership, pregnancy/maternity, or sexual orientation.




We are an inclusive organization and actively promote equality of opportunity for all with the right mix of talent, skills and potential. We welcome all applications from a wide range of candidates. Selection for roles will be based on individual merit alone.”

description:
About the job

About Turing:

Based in San Francisco, California, Turing is the world’s leading research accelerator for frontier AI labs and a trusted partner for global enterprises deploying advanced AI systems.

Turing supports customers in two ways: first, by accelerating frontier research with high-quality data, advanced training pipelines, plus top AI researchers who specialize in coding, reasoning, STEM, multilinguality, multimodality, and agents; and second, by applying that expertise to help enterprises transform AI from proof of concept into proprietary intelligence with systems that perform reliably, deliver measurable impact, and drive lasting results on the P&L




About the Role:

We are looking for a detail-oriented Business Analyst to support evaluation and annotation workflows focused on quality, accuracy, and structured reasoning. This role is ideal for someone who is comfortable working with digital tools, performing side-by-side evaluations, and providing clear, well-supported justifications based on fact-checking.




Responsibilities:

Perform side-by-side (SxS) evaluations on GHD, comparing outputs and assessing quality, accuracy, and relevance.
Conduct fact-checking to validate information and identify inconsistencies or inaccuracies.
Provide detailed, well-structured justifications to support evaluation decisions.
Follow established evaluation guidelines and quality standards consistently.
Use browser-based tools and extensions to support analysis and review tasks.
Execute simple command-line instructions when required to support evaluation workflows.
Document findings clearly and communicate insights to stakeholders when needed.




Requirements:

Solid comfort using a computer in a professional setting.
Experience working with browser extensions and online tools.
Ability to run basic command-line commands (no advanced programming required).
Strong analytical and critical thinking skills.
High attention to detail and ability to justify decisions clearly and objectively.
Comfortable working with evaluation frameworks, comparisons, and structured analysis.
Good written communication skills in English.




Nice to Have:

Previous experience with evaluation, annotation, QA, or audit-type work.
Familiarity with data quality, content evaluation, or model/output assessments.
Background in business analysis, operations, or process improvement.




Perks of Freelancing With Turing:

Competitive compensation based on experience and expertise.
Flexible working hours and a fully remote environment.
Opportunity to contribute to impactful research initiatives.
Potential for contract extension based on performance and project needs.




Offer Details:

Commitment Required: 30 hours per week minimum.
Engagement Type: Contractor/Freelancer (no medical or paid leave).
Time Overlap: Some overlap with UTC-8:00 (2–5 hrs/day) America/Los_Angeles.




Application Process:

Shortlisted candidates will be invited to complete an analytical challenge.
Once the assessment is cleared, you’re ready to start contributing!




After applying, you will receive an email with a login link. Please use that link to access the portal and complete your profile.




Know amazing talent? Refer them at turing.com/referrals, and earn money from your network.

description:
About the job

Do work that matters. 

At AlertMedia, everything we do supports our mission: To save lives and minimize loss by identifying active threats globally and facilitating the timely communications when an emergency threatens personal safety and business continuity. 

Our core values drive us in our important mission of keeping people safe & informed: 


We’re humans not robots 
Customers always come first 
We work better together 
Simplicity is our strength 
Our reputation is priceless 
Hard work pays off 


The Customer Success Specialist will take ownership of managing customer relationships, which we value above all else. This role will focus on emerging customers and partner closely with our Customer Support Team to foster success. In this key role, working closely with Customer Success, Customer Support, Marketing and Sales teams, they are responsible for driving engagement to support customer health, retention, and growth. The right candidate will be determined to achieve effective customer enablement, as well as the highest possible satisfaction rates across the accounts they manage. They will make sure customer needs are always met and problems are solved quickly. 

As one of the fastest growing software companies in the nation, we’re focused on finding the best talent and building the best team to continue accelerating our rapid growth to keep up with our demand! 

Who you are: 

You'd be excited to buy into our "customer love" mentality and are motivated by going above and beyond for the customers you serve. You are a team player, an individual contributor and have a roll-up-your-sleeves mentality when it comes to your customers and there is no task too big or too small to best support them. You enjoy building relationships, community engagement, and driving adoption of all features of the AlertMedia platform. 

What you get to do every day: 


Support our customers in the SMB segment as a product expert to drive adoption and enable them to accomplish their goals 
Manage customer touch points with automated processes and innovative approaches 
Host customer webinars and facilitate customer collaboration 
Align with the Customer Success organization to support initiatives to grow and expand existing accounts through adoption, cross-sell, and upsell opportunities 
Evaluate health and assess risk for your book of business 
Partner with our Customer Support team to manage escalations requiring more strategic oversight 
Respond to all customer inquiries in a timely manner, within 1 business day 
Effectively track all customer communication and data in Gainsight and Salesforce 
Acquire industry knowledge related to general trends, use cases, and emerging technologies 
Ensure positive and productive customer experience to maintain software renewals 
Contribute to AlertMedia’s culture, values, and vision for the future 
This is an individual contributor role and not a manager role 


What you bring to the role: 


2+ years’ experience in customer success management or account management 
Experience in a Saas or Tech environment strongly preferred 
Salesforce or Hubspot experience preferred 
Strong organization and time management skills with ability to prioritize 
Comfort level managing a large volume of accounts 
Outstanding verbal and written communication skills 
Willingness to test and implement innovative ideas 
Experience with customer implementation and software adoption preferred 
Strong listening skills with  the ability to mirror and work with a variety of customer profiles 
Ability to work with all members of our team (customer support, business development, software development, and product management) 
Must be independent, self-motivated, success-driven, and adaptable 
The ability and desire to work in a fast-paced, challenging environment 
A vision for efficiency 
The desire to learn and be coached 
Bilingual English/Spanish a must 
This is an in office, hybrid position (2 days a week) in our downtown Mexico City (Reforma) offices 


Location: This is a hybrid position based in Mexico City.

Sponsorship: AlertMedia does not sponsor

Why you’ll love working at AlertMedia:


Competitive Base Salary
Annual Company Bonus
100% Employer-Paid Medical, Dental, and Vision Coverage
Parental Leave Program 
Christmas Bonus (Aguinaldo)
Generous Vacation and Holiday Policy
Monthly Work-from-Home Stipend
All Necessary Equipment Provided: Laptop, monitor and accessories
Recognition & Rewards: We celebrate wins big and small
Language Immersion Program with employees spread out across the globe (English, Spanish, etc...depending on your interest)
Continuous Learning & Growth: From mentorship and tech talks to personalized 1:1 career development sessions, we’re deeply committed to investing in your professional journey
Award Winning Culture: We’re passionate about the meaningful work we do—and even more grateful for the incredible people we get to do it with!


About AlertMedia:

AlertMedia helps organizations protect their people and businesses through all phases of an emergency. Our award-winning threat intelligence, emergency communication, and travel risk management solutions help companies of all sizes identify, respond to, and recover from critical events faster and more confidently. AlertMedia supports essential communication for thousands of leading businesses—including JetBlue, Coca-Cola Bottling, and Walmart—in more than 150 countries.

We are an equal opportunity employer focused on creating a collaborative and exciting place for all to work. Ensuring a diverse, inclusive, and equitable workplace for all people is key to our success and core to our values. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

This is an amazing opportunity to be part of our wave of momentum and take our company, and your career, to the next growth stage. We'd love to get to know you better and share how we serve our amazing customers. For more information, please visit www.alertmedia.com.

Come join us in our mission to save lives and minimize loss through effective communication.

By applying for the role, you agree that Alert Media will use your Personal Information in connection with the recruitment process and in accordance with our Privacy Policy – https://www.alertmedia.com/legal/privacy-policy/
