description:
About the job

HireLatam is a premier recruitment agency that places top Latin American talent in independent contractor roles in US companies. With a proven track record and a commitment to excellence, we're your trusted partner in the pursuit of career success. Our extensive network, personalized approach, and supportive guidance ensure that you're in the best hands to find your next job opportunity.

 

Job Title: Remote Customer Experience Lead - Cleaning Services (100% Work From Home) 

 

Location: Remote from Latin America

 

Position Type: Full Time

 

Salary: $1,700 USD/month + incentives/bonus compensation

 

Schedule: Monday-Friday, 7:30 A.M to 4:30 P.M, Central Day Time (CDT).

 

 

Position Overview:

 

Our Client provides premium, recurring residential cleaning and specialty home services to high-end homeowners throughout the United States. Operating with professionally trained field employees, advanced tracking software, and an unwavering commitment to operational excellence, they have built a reputation for delivering a luxury-tier customer experience. They are scaling quickly and are focused on establishing a robust, high-performance customer support ecosystem.

 

The Role

 

Our Client is seeking a high-caliber Customer Experience & Sales Coordinator who serves as the primary point of contact for customers and prospective customers. This role is responsible for front-line customer communication, customer scheduling, lead follow-up, quoting, sales support, and timely, professional communication throughout the customer relationship.

This position reports to the Customer Experience Leader and works closely with the Operations team. Customer Service owns the customer relationship and communication, while Operations owns service delivery, scheduling strategy, quality, and field execution.

The Customer Experience Leader provides coaching, process guidance, and support for escalated customer situations, complex sales opportunities, and SOP ownership.

The ideal candidate enjoys helping people, solving problems, staying organized, and following through. They communicate proactively, work well inside structured systems, and take pride in making sure customers know what is happening and what to expect.

 

Key Responsibilities

 

Customer Experience & Retention

• Serve as the primary customer contact through phone, text, email, and the customer portal

• Build strong relationships with customers, particularly recurring customers

• Respond quickly and professionally to customer inquiries

• Take ownership of customer communication from initial request through final resolution

• Follow up proactively to ensure customer satisfaction

• Identify customers who may be at risk of cancelling and proactively work to retain them

• Understand and document the root cause of customer concerns

• Coordinate with the Customer Experience Leader and Operations team to implement solutions

• Escalate operational issues when appropriate while maintaining ownership of customer communication until the issue is resolved

• Generate customer reviews and referrals through exceptional service

Scheduling & Customer Coordination

• Schedule new recurring customers into available openings identified by the Operations team

• Coordinate onboarding of new recurring customers

• Process customer schedule changes, skips, and routine service requests

• Place customers into existing schedule openings when capacity is clearly available

• Assist with route optimization by using available openings and communicating opportunities to Operations

• Identify upcoming scheduling conflicts and coordinate with the Operations team to determine the best solution

• Communicate scheduling updates promptly and accurately to customers

• Maintain accurate customer records and scheduling information within MaidCentral or similar systems

Sales, Lead Management & Quoting

• Respond promptly to inbound sales inquiries by phone, text, email, and website leads

• Schedule walkthrough appointments and consultations for larger homes and more complex opportunities

• Prepare and provide quotes for one-time cleanings, move-in/move-out cleanings, apartments, townhomes, small homes, and other straightforward

opportunities

• Prepare and provide quotes for specialty cleaning services and recommend appropriate service packages

• Follow up consistently with prospects and unsold estimates

• Convert qualified leads into recurring customers when appropriate

• Recommend appropriate recurring service frequencies and cleaning packages

• Identify opportunities to expand customer relationships through recurring and specialty cleaning services

• Maintain accurate lead and customer information within MaidCentral or similar systems

• Partner with the Customer Experience Leader on more complex estimates and sales opportunities as needed

Administrative Support & Process Execution

• Maintain accurate customer records, notes, preferences, and service history within MaidCentral

• Assist customers with account updates, billing questions, customer portal support, payment questions, and service changes

• Support administrative follow-up to ensure open customer items are closed out

• Assist the Customer Experience Leader with SOP implementation, documentation updates, and process consistency

• Support marketing campaigns, customer communications, and administrative initiatives

• Demonstrate flexibility and willingness to assist wherever needed to support the customer experience and company goals

 

Qualifications, Skills, and Key Competencies

 

Required Qualifications

 

 Fluent in English and Spanish
• Customer service or sales experience within home services
• Experience using sales, scheduling, and CRM software
• Strong verbal and written communication skills
• Strong customer service, relationship-building, and sales skills
• Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail
• Demonstrated problem-solving ability and sound judgment
• Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment
• Comfortable working independently in a remote environment

 

Preferred Qualifications

• Residential cleaning industry experience

• Experience using MaidCentral

• Customer retention experience

• Inside sales or customer sales experience

• Experience preparing service quotes or estimates

• Experience quoting specialty cleaning or other home services

• Scheduling or dispatch experience

• Experience supporting recurring service businesses

• Experience handling escalated customer concerns

• Experience working remotely with distributed teams

 

Benefits

 

Competitive Compensation Architecture: A reliable base salary of $2,000 to $3,000 USD per month, supplemented by performance-based incentive structures.
High-Impact Autonomy: Step out of the traditional call center matrix into a strategic, leadership-focused role where your execution directly preserves corporate revenue.
100% Remote Infrastructure: Long-term, secure full-time contract alignment managed entirely from the comfort of a tailored home office.

title: Customer Success & Onboarding Specialist — Sales Track
skills: You are proficient in Troubleshooting, Software as a service (SaaS), Customer retention, Client onboarding, Account management.
You have the potential to develop in Marketing automation, Hospitality, Event management, CRM tools, Artificial intelligence (AI).
English - Fully fluent
Joaquin Corrales - Founder at VenueAI | Building the AI operating system for wedding and event venues
Ericka - Chief Operating Officer at VenueAI
description:
About the job

I’m helping VenueAI find a top candidate to join their team full-time for the role of Customer Success & Onboarding Specialist — Sales Track.




You'll empower small businesses with AI, owning client success from onboarding to revenue growth.




Compensation:

USD 36K - 42K/year

+ Commissions (:USD5K/year)




Location:

Remote: Mexico




Mission of VenueAI:

"Our mission is to help venues and event spaces grow by using AI to make client communications smarter, faster, and more effective. We empower our customers to book more events, deliver better experiences, and focus on what they do best — creating unforgettable moments."




What makes you a strong candidate:

You are proficient in Troubleshooting, Software as a service (SaaS), Customer retention, Client onboarding, Account management.
You have the potential to develop in Marketing automation, Hospitality, Event management, CRM tools, Artificial intelligence (AI).
English - Fully fluent




Responsibilities and more:

VenueAI is a fast-growing SaaS company helping wedding and event venues book more events using AI. Our clients are relationship-driven small business owners, and the person in this role becomes one of their most trusted partners.




We're looking for someone who combines exceptional communication skills with strong technical aptitude. You'll own the onboarding experience end-to-end: leading client calls, managing the setup process yourself, and ensuring every new customer launches successfully and starts seeing results quickly. Beyond onboarding, you'll continue supporting clients through adoption, retention, renewals, and future growth opportunities, helping them feel confident and successful with VenueAI.




You won't be expected to be an engineer, and our technical team is available for more complex issues. However, you should be comfortable handling the day-to-day technical aspects of onboarding independently. As you deepen your product knowledge and strengthen client relationships, you'll have a clear path to expand into product demos, identifying expansion opportunities, and playing a larger role in revenue growth over time.




===WHAT YOU'LL DO===

* Serve as the warm, confident face of VenueAI during onboarding and client interactions




* Own the onboarding process end-to-end, including leading calls and managing technical setup such as integrations, forms, DNS configuration, and troubleshooting




* Ensure clients go live quickly and successfully by conducting quality assurance checks before launch




* Guide customers through the early adoption phase so they understand the product, realize value quickly, and feel confident using VenueAI




* Build strong client relationships that drive retention, renewals, and long-term success




* Identify opportunities for upgrades, expanded usage, and other growth initiatives as clients achieve success




* Develop deep product expertise to confidently address client questions and provide strategic guidance




* Partner with the technical team on complex issues while independently managing routine setup and support needs




* Grow into leading product demos and contributing to revenue-generating activities over time




* Identify friction points in onboarding and the customer journey, and help improve processes as we scale




===WHO YOU ARE===

* Naturally engaging and relationship-oriented; people trust and connect with you quickly




* Fluent or near-native in English, with strong verbal communication skills and confidence in client-facing video calls




* Technically capable and comfortable managing onboarding tasks such as integrations, forms, DNS setup, and basic troubleshooting without relying heavily on developers




* Consultative in your approach, focused on listening, building rapport, and guiding clients toward success




* Organized, proactive, and able to work independently in a fast-paced environment




* Experienced, with 2+ years in SaaS Customer Success, Implementation, Onboarding, or Account Management, ideally supporting US-based clients




* Aware that exceptional onboarding drives adoption, retention, renewals, and future revenue opportunities




* Excited by a role that combines customer success with a long-term path toward demos and sales responsibilities




===NICE TO HAVE===

* Experience transitioning from Customer Success or Onboarding into a demo or sales-focused role




* Familiarity with CRM platforms, marketing automation tools, or AI products




* Experience in the events, hospitality, or wedding industries




===THIS ROLE IS NOT FOR YOU IF===

* You prefer working exclusively behind the scenes and avoid client-facing interactions




* You don't enjoy building relationships and engaging directly with customers




* You depend on developers to resolve routine setup or integration tasks




* You're uncomfortable troubleshooting issues and finding solutions independently




* You want to remain strictly in a support role with no interest in demos, sales, or revenue-related responsibilities




===COMPENSATION & GROWTH===

* Base salary: USD $36,000–$42,000/year, depending on experience




* Performance bonus tied to onboarding success, retention, and client outcomes




* 100% remote, exclusive full-time role




* A clear growth path from onboarding → customer success → demos → sales as VenueAI continues to scale




Your potential leader(s):

Joaquin Corrales - Founder at VenueAI | Building the AI operating system for wedding and event venues
Ericka - Chief Operating Officer at VenueAI

title: Strategic Sourcing Profesional Services

FedEx Express is committed to leveraging advanced technology to solve business problems and enhance operational efficiency. Our culture fosters innovation, collaboration, and a dedication to excellence.

description:
About the job

This position is 100% remote for Mexico, Columbia, Venezuela and Guatemala, Argentina, Honduras, DR, Brazil applicants. Not a US based post. The hours are 10am-7pm MST and includes one Saturday a month from 8-5 MST.

About Us

The Savings Group (TSG), the parent company of RateGenius, AUTOPAY and Tresl, is the most diversified consumer marketplace for automotive finance, refinance, and protection plans. Through a network of more than 180 lenders across all 50 states, TSG provides consumers more choices for their origination and refinanced auto loans, while also delivering loan volume growth to its lender network and partners through a proprietary, web-based platform.

Why Work at The Savings Group?

Working at TSG is more than your standard J-O-B. That's because we work with some of the most amazing goal-oriented and driven people in fintech and still embody the spirit of a startup! Not only have we worked together to perfect the way we serve our customers, but we've built a pretty awesome culture in the process. Our people come from all kinds of backgrounds to create something exciting and new: A BETTER WAY TO DO CAR LOANS. We offer steady growth, a strong and supportive leadership team, and in-depth training and tools to ensure your success.

Summary

The Customer Success Representative Level I ensures that customers receive wonderful customer service in all aspects when taking care of the customers. This includes any and all customers that are calling in to inquire regarding potential vehicle purchases, refinances or questions regarding open applications and so forth. Customer Success maintains all overflow through TSG phone systems and is the start of the TSG journey for all our customers throughout all of its brands. Customer Success Representatives Level I are required to answer all phone calls received, respond to customer inquiries, complete customer applications either over the phone or via text/email, and send emails to the correct corresponding departments as needed to resolve customer complaints. This includes internal transfers as well as maintaining Legal and Compliance concerns when completing applications as well. 

ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES include achieving positive results in the following key areas:


Understand and demonstrate principles of The Savings Group’s Mission, Vision, and Values. 
Document all account, contact, and activity information into various systems. 
Answer calls from internal and external sources, while providing a world-class customer service experience. 
Complete applications by utilizing various systems to obtain the necessary information and consent
Manage inbound and outbound communication by email, phone and/ or text to customers to complete applications
Ensure to send communications to the correct departments when the customer calls in for something that goes beyond the scope of what you are able to assist with (i.e, titles issues, escalations, payoff issues, loan specialist request etc..)
Consistently respond to auto finance inquiries in a timely, efficient, and professional manner. 
Meet all metrics and standards set forth by management
Additional duties as required. 


QUALIFICATIONS

To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

Knowledge, Skills, And Abilities


Education: High School diploma is required. One (1) year of customer service-related experience in a call center preferred
Experience: Experience in inside phone sales, telemarketing, quality assurance or customer service experience with auto finance companies or, car dealerships
Language/Communication/Interpersonal: Strong listening, verbal, and written communication skills. Ability to read and comprehend simple instructions, short correspondence, and memos. Ability to write simple correspondence. Ability to effectively present information to customers, clients, and all levels of management
Reasoning Ability: Ability to apply common sense understanding to carry out detailed but uninvolved written or oral instructions. Ability to deal with problems involving concrete variables in standardized situations
Analytical: Detail-oriented with ability to organize, prioritize and work independently. Strong critical thinking skills
Technical: Ability to toggle between applications on a dual monitor setup. Proficiency with Microsoft Office, with intermediate Excel ability, required. Keyboarding/computer skills, heavy computer and phone usage
Other: High level of personal motivation, professionalism and integrity required. Ability to exercise discretion and judgment. Must be dependable


Computer IT/Requirements/compensation:

Must have a desktop or laptop PC capable of installing and running the Windows App. Device must support hardwired (Ethernet) connection for stable network access.


Reliable internet connection with minimum speeds of:
Download: 50 Mbps or higher. Upload: 10 Mbps or higher
Must provide their own headset, mouse, and keyboard
IT can only provide very limited support for personal devices; it is the responsibility of the new hire to run updates and maintain their personal device
$1500 US Dollars per month paid on the last day of each month. 


Job description statements are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by employees assigned to this job title. They are not intended to be construed as an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties, and skills required.

The Savings Group (including all its subsidiaries: AUTOPAY, RateGenius Loan Services, Inc., and Innovative Funding Services dba Tresl) is an equal opportunity employer. With regard to hiring and promotions, qualified persons will not be denied employment opportunity based on race, color, national origin, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, age 40 and over, disability, military status, or genetic information. They proudly serve more than 10 million U.S. households with more than 19 million individual policies across all 50 states through the efforts of over 48,000 exclusive and independent agents and nearly 18,500 employees. Finally, our Client is part of one the largest Insurance Groups in the world.

About The Role

We are seeking an Operations Analyst to support Workforce Management (WFM) and real-time operations within our contact center environment. description:
About the job

Persona is where the world’s best companies and the world’s best talent meet.




We place exceptional people from 98 countries into full-time, long-term remote roles with the most ambitious companies on earth. Fortune 500 enterprises, fast-growing startups backed by Sequoia, Founders Fund, and Andreessen Horowitz, and high-performing companies of every size in between. Project management, operations, finance, design, marketing, customer success, and more. One person, one dedicated client, built to last.




The bar is the highest in the industry. More than 200,000 people apply every month, and fewer than 1 in 2,000 are accepted. If you make it, you join a network of people who have cleared a standard most companies couldn’t recruit for on their own, and you get the kind of role that’s almost impossible to find on your own: industry-leading pay set against your experience and skill, real career growth, and a stable seat at a serious company, working from home, anywhere in the world.




Persona was founded in 2018 by two Stanford graduates on a simple idea: talent is everywhere, but the systems that connect talent to opportunity are broken. We’re rebuilding them, and we’ve grown into one of the fastest-growing companies in the global talent industry doing it.




We hire people who care about the craft of their work, take ownership without being asked, and want to build a career rather than just hold a job. If that’s you, we’d like to meet you.




Role Summary

We are hiring a Customer Service Professional to support the Personal Lines book of business at an independent U.S. insurance agency. Renewals of existing policies are the lifeblood of an insurance agency, and this role focuses primarily on serving existing customers — managing inquiries, supporting renewals, maintaining accurate records, and helping retain and grow each policyholder relationship. This role is purpose-built for someone who knows the rhythms of an agency, understands personal lines coverage (auto, home, renters, umbrella, and related products), and can confidently navigate carrier portals and agency management systems. Ideal candidates take personal ownership of every customer interaction and treat retention and renewal work as a craft.




Key Responsibilities

Serve as the agency's primary customer-facing point of contact for existing Personal Lines customers
Take personal ownership for resolving customer inquiries through to closure
Master and execute all assigned customer service workflows consistently and accurately

Through both behavior and communication, instill confidence in customers that their request is being handled

Maintain meticulous customer records in the agency management system, including coverage details, communication logs, and document attachments
Coordinate with carriers on underwriting questions, billing issues, and claims escalations
Complete administrative and agency systems upkeep tasks (data hygiene, document scanning, file organization)
Contribute to retention metrics by delivering an exceptional service experience that drives renewal and referral




Required Qualifications

2+ years of experience in a customer service, account management, or service representative role
Direct experience with Personal Lines insurance (auto, home, renters, umbrella, and related personal lines products) — including coverage basics, endorsement processing, and renewal workflows
Hands-on experience with an agency management system (AMS360, Applied Epic, EZLynx, HawkSoft, QQCatalyst, or similar)
Familiarity with carrier portals and standard personal lines carriers (Progressive, Travelers, Safeco, Nationwide, Liberty Mutual, or comparable)
Strong attention to detail and high standard for accuracy across customer records, policy documents, and system entries
Strong critical thinking and problem-solving skills
Excellent organizational and time-management skills; able to manage multiple projects and assignments simultaneously
Strong interpersonal skills (both written and verbal)
Ability to embrace change and work in a fast-paced environment
Fully fluent in English

Ability to commit long-term and full-time




Preferred Qualifications

Active Property & Casualty (P&C) insurance license, or willingness and ability to obtain one

Multi-state personal lines experience

title: Senior Customer Success Specialist
skills: Job Title: Senior Customer Success Specialist – Budgeting & Reporting
Work Hours: Part-Time (20 hours per week), transition into a Full-Time (40 hours per week) position within 3 to 6 months
Schedule/Timezone: Days of the week to be determined, 9:00 AM – 1:00 PM (with flexibility) / PST
Location: 100% Remote (Latin America Only)
Conduct live, consultative Zoom and Microsoft Teams support and training sessions with corporate finance users
Guide customers through critical financial software workflows, including budget roll forwards, forecast creation, payroll forecasting, allocations, and ERP data exports/imports
Efficiently respond to incoming customer emails and technical support tickets regarding workflow and reporting concerns
Support customer onboarding initiatives, shadow senior team members on demo calls, and help clients implement operational best practices
Update customer-facing Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), author clear help topics and FAQ documentation, and log recurring issues to optimize internal support processes
Strong, bilingual spoken and written communication skills in both English and Spanish
Minimum of 3 years of hands-on experience working directly with financial budgeting or reporting software platforms
A polished, professional customer presence with the confidence to lead live screenshare troubleshooting sessions and ask clarifying questions
Deep understanding of the operational challenges involved in managing large, complex corporate budgets
Direct experience creating SOPs, training materials, or technical knowledge base articles
Highly proficient with Microsoft Excel, standard ticketing/helpdesk systems, and virtual meeting tools (Zoom/Teams)
A reliable high-speed internet connection, a quiet dedicated home office environment, a functional webcam, and a dual-monitor setup
Prior support experience specifically tailored to ERP systems or mid-market accounting software
Hands-on experience utilizing industry-standard helpdesk software such as Zendesk, Freshdesk, or Zoho Desk

Familiarity or exposure to specialized financial reporting tools, business intelligence platforms, or budgeting software (e.g., Power BI, SQL, Solver, Jet Reports, Velixo, Vena, Prophix, Planful, Adaptive Planning, Datarails, Martus, or Forecaster)
description:
About the job

Job Title: Senior Customer Success Specialist – Budgeting & Reporting
Work Hours: Part-Time (20 hours per week), transition into a Full-Time (40 hours per week) position within 3 to 6 months
Schedule/Timezone: Days of the week to be determined, 9:00 AM – 1:00 PM (with flexibility) / PST
Location: 100% Remote (Latin America Only)




Overview




Are you a finance or accounting professional who loves solving software puzzles and helping clients succeed?




We are a leading financial budgeting, forecasting, and reporting software provider designed specifically to empower mid-market corporate finance teams. Operating at the intersection of FinTech and Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP), we specialize in creating seamless software solutions that integrate with major accounting ecosystems like Microsoft Dynamics, Sage Intacct, Oracle NetSuite, and Acumatica. Our mission is to eliminate the headache of complex financial planning for CFOs, Controllers, and Finance Managers across diverse industries, including healthcare, insurance, and nonprofits.




In this role, you won't be reading from a scripted call-center prompt. Instead, you will act as a true consultative partner, leveraging your financial acumen to guide users through intricate software workflows, troubleshoot complex reporting queries, and help organizations manage massive operations smoothly.




Key Responsibilities




Conduct live, consultative Zoom and Microsoft Teams support and training sessions with corporate finance users
Guide customers through critical financial software workflows, including budget roll forwards, forecast creation, payroll forecasting, allocations, and ERP data exports/imports
Efficiently respond to incoming customer emails and technical support tickets regarding workflow and reporting concerns
Support customer onboarding initiatives, shadow senior team members on demo calls, and help clients implement operational best practices
Update customer-facing Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), author clear help topics and FAQ documentation, and log recurring issues to optimize internal support processes




Must-Have Requirements




Strong, bilingual spoken and written communication skills in both English and Spanish
Minimum of 3 years of hands-on experience working directly with financial budgeting or reporting software platforms
A polished, professional customer presence with the confidence to lead live screenshare troubleshooting sessions and ask clarifying questions
Deep understanding of the operational challenges involved in managing large, complex corporate budgets
Direct experience creating SOPs, training materials, or technical knowledge base articles
Highly proficient with Microsoft Excel, standard ticketing/helpdesk systems, and virtual meeting tools (Zoom/Teams)
A reliable high-speed internet connection, a quiet dedicated home office environment, a functional webcam, and a dual-monitor setup




Nice-to-Have Requirements




Prior support experience specifically tailored to ERP systems or mid-market accounting software
Hands-on experience utilizing industry-standard helpdesk software such as Zendesk, Freshdesk, or Zoho Desk
Familiarity or exposure to specialized financial reporting tools, business intelligence platforms, or budgeting software (e.g., Power BI, SQL, Solver, Jet Reports, Velixo, Vena, Prophix, Planful, Adaptive Planning, Datarails, Martus, or Forecaster)




Why Join Us




This is an exceptional entry point into an innovative tech space that values your accounting and financial expertise. We believe in setting our team up for long-term success, which is why you will receive comprehensive, hands-on training—from shadowing live support calls to practicing in dedicated demo environments before taking full ownership of client accounts.

title: CUSTOMER SERVICE SPECIALIST II
skills: Serve as the central point of contact for assigned accounts, providing timely responses and fostering proactive communication.
Build and maintain strong relationships through regular customer interactions, formal feedback surveys, and tailored meetings.
Act as a liaison between customers and internal teams to resolve escalations, propose solutions, and drive process improvements.
Process customer orders, changes, returns, and quotes in SAP
Review and ensure compliance with customer terms, quality requirements, and contractual obligations.
Oversee scheduling agreements, monitor critical shipments, and expedite orders as required.
Drive initiatives for Extraordinary Customer Experience (ECE) and satisfaction metrics like CES, FCR, and NPS.
Conduct root-cause analysis of issues and implement corrective actions to improve service delivery.
Identify opportunities for streamlining processes and execute small improvement projects.
Address technical inquiries using internal resources and provide alternative solutions when necessary.
Investigate and resolve customer issues related to delivery, price, and quality.
Collaborate with Key Account Managers and other stakeholders to meet on-time delivery and operational goals.
Represent the "voice of the customer" to internal teams, facilitating effective communication and alignment.
Participate in training sessions, process improvement initiatives, and cross-departmental projects.
Mentor and support new team members to enhance team capabilities.
Bachelor’s degree preferred or minimum 3 years of experience in a customer service/account management role, preferably in a B2B industrial environment.
Proficiency in Microsoft 365 tools (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, etc.) and SAP or other ERP systems.
Strong interpersonal, communication, and organizational skills with an emphasis on customer advocacy.
Demonstrated ability to manage multiple tasks, meet tight deadlines, and proactive prioritize work to ensure successful outcomes.
Excellent analytical skills and process improvement focus, including familiarity with Lean principles.
Flexibility to travel 10-25% of the time for customer meetings, audits, and business reviews.
Exceptional time management and attention to detail.
Strong collaboration and teamwork abilities.
Ability to handle high-pressure situations with professionalism and efficiency.
Proven problem-solving and negotiation skills.
Commitment to delivering extraordinary customer experience through proactive and innovative service delivery.
description:
About the job

At TE, you will unleash your potential working with people from diverse backgrounds and industries to create a safer, sustainable and more connected world.

Job Overview

Customer Care Professional (CCP) is a key role focused on building strong customer relationships, ensuring overall satisfaction, and driving continuous improvements in customer experience. As the primary point of contact between TE Connectivity and its customers, the CCP manages a wide range of pre-sales and post-sales activities.

This role is responsible for handling customer inquiries, processing and managing orders, and resolving complex issues efficiently and effectively. The CCP partners closely with cross-functional teams to deliver excellent service, strengthen customer loyalty, and support business performance.

In addition to day-to-day customer engagement, the CCP plays an active role in identifying opportunities to improve processes and enhance the overall customer experience.

This position may involve working with technical data subject to export control regulations and requires compliance with all applicable laws and company policies.

Job Requirements

Customer Engagement and Relationship Management:


 Serve as the central point of contact for assigned accounts, providing timely responses and fostering proactive communication.
 Build and maintain strong relationships through regular customer interactions, formal feedback surveys, and tailored meetings.
 Act as a liaison between customers and internal teams to resolve escalations, propose solutions, and drive process improvements.


Order And Service Management


 Process customer orders, changes, returns, and quotes in SAP
 Review and ensure compliance with customer terms, quality requirements, and contractual obligations.
 Oversee scheduling agreements, monitor critical shipments, and expedite orders as required.


Customer Satisfaction And Performance Enhancement


 Drive initiatives for Extraordinary Customer Experience (ECE) and satisfaction metrics like CES, FCR, and NPS.
 Conduct root-cause analysis of issues and implement corrective actions to improve service delivery.
 Identify opportunities for streamlining processes and execute small improvement projects.


Technical Support And Problem Resolution


 Address technical inquiries using internal resources and provide alternative solutions when necessary.
 Investigate and resolve customer issues related to delivery, price, and quality.
 Collaborate with Key Account Managers and other stakeholders to meet on-time delivery and operational goals.


Cross-Functional Collaboration And Continuous Improvement


 Represent the "voice of the customer" to internal teams, facilitating effective communication and alignment.
 Participate in training sessions, process improvement initiatives, and cross-departmental projects.
 Mentor and support new team members to enhance team capabilities.


What Your Background Should Look Like

Required Qualifications and Skills:


 Bachelor’s degree preferred or minimum 3 years of experience in a customer service/account management role, preferably in a B2B industrial environment.
 Proficiency in Microsoft 365 tools (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, etc.) and SAP or other ERP systems.
 Strong interpersonal, communication, and organizational skills with an emphasis on customer advocacy.
 Demonstrated ability to manage multiple tasks, meet tight deadlines, and proactive prioritize work to ensure successful outcomes.
 Excellent analytical skills and process improvement focus, including familiarity with Lean principles.
 Flexibility to travel 10-25% of the time for customer meetings, audits, and business reviews.


Competencies and Key Attributes


 Exceptional time management and attention to detail.
 Strong collaboration and teamwork abilities.
 Ability to handle high-pressure situations with professionalism and efficiency.
 Proven problem-solving and negotiation skills.
 Commitment to delivering extraordinary customer experience through proactive and innovative service delivery.


Competencies

Values: Integrity, Accountability, Inclusion, Innovation, Teamwork

description:
About the job

At TE, you will unleash your potential working with people from diverse backgrounds and industries to create a safer, sustainable and more connected world. 

Job Overview

This role is a frontline service position providing assistance to TE’s internal and external customers. Overall expectations is to provide outstanding service and strive to resolve customer’s requests in a timely manner. Performance expectations are to meet or exceed Operations and Quality standards.

Ensures effective delivery of service in the support of internal and external customers through regular interaction with customers and other functional areas via telephone, email, chat or through other social media platforms to resolve product/services issues fairly, effectively and in accordance with the consumer laws.

Develop and manage customer intimacy to named accounts to deliver extraordinary customer experiences

Job Requirements 


Ensures all responses follow approved company guidelines
Duties and tasks are more complex and less routine
May creatively adapt procedures, techniques, and/or equipment to meet job requirements
Handling customer concerns by providing appropriate solutions and alternatives, and then following up to ensure resolution
Resolve, prioritize and research customer issues in a timely and accurate fashion.
Work in partnership with our customers and Account Managers to plan activity and grow and develop the accounts
Understand customer needs and requirements by identifying and seeking out new opportunities to improve the service level and relationship
Understanding the customers' business and competition
Proactively identifying and eliminating potential issues that may negatively affect the customer.
Optimize communications and relationships between Customer Service Advisory Services Support and various other departments and parties involved 
Liaise and coordinate with Account Managers and internal staff proactively in relation to the day to day running of the account(s)
CCP ensures that all customer queries, (internal and external) on product quality, availability, and substitutions, inventory and order status, pricing, billing, invoices and traffic carrier concerns are resolved satisfactorily
Analyze and lead improvement to Customer’s specific KPI’s
Align with support team of specialists to ensure tasks are achieved with quick turnaround
Representing and advocating internally for the voice of customer
Answering incoming calls regarding service questions, product problems, billing issues, and general client concern
Keeping detailed and adequate records of customer interactions, transactions, comments, and complaints
Performs contract review of all orders for customer requirements to ensure that the Associate Representative (i.e., Order Administration) is processing orders per negotiated requirements including importing and exporting requirements
Support process improvement to standardized operational procedures (SOP)
Works under limited or minimal supervision, referring only very complex issues to higher levels
May provide training and guidance to others
Process purchase orders and complete the follow-up process, meaning ensuring that orders are delivered on time and in the correct manner.
Process quotations following the standard procedure, meaning responding to the customer within 48 hours with the price and the corresponding document.
Process RMAs for customers in a timely and proper manner, and also ensure and follow up until the parts are returned to the customer, or if applicable, that the corresponding credit is issued.
Review the customer's backlog at least twice a week to verify that there are no changes in the order dates and to be able to anticipate any changes.
Be the customer's first point of contact and assist with their requests, even if they pertain to another business unit or department

What Your Background Should Look Like


Relevant work experience (3+ years)
SAP or Salesforce system knowledge is required
Proficiency with MS Office applications
Ability to work in a fast pace ever-changing environment
Analytical skills and problem-solving capabilities
Advanced English (specifically in speaking and reading comprehension)
Ability to retain a lot of information


Competencies

Values: Integrity, Accountability, Inclusion, Innovation, Teamwork

About Te Connectivity

TE Connectivity plc (NYSE: TEL) is a global industrial technology leader creating a safer, sustainable, productive, and connected future. As a trusted innovation partner, our broad range of connectivity and sensor solutions enable the distribution of power, signal and data to advance next-generation transportation, energy networks, automated factories, data centers enabling artificial intelligence, and more.

Our more than 90,000 employees, including 10,000 engineers, work alongside customers in approximately 130 countries. In a world that is racing ahead, TE ensures that EVERY CONNECTION COUNTS. Learn more at www.te.com and on LinkedIn, Facebook, WeChat, Instagram and X (formerly Twitter).

What Te Connectivity Offers

We are pleased to offer you an exciting total package that can also be flexibly adapted to changing life situations - the well-being of our employees is our top priority!


 Competitive Salary Package
 Performance-Based Bonus Plans
 Health and Wellness Incentives
 Employee Stock Purchase Program
 Community Outreach Programs / Charity Events
 Employee Resource Group


IMPORTANT NOTICE REGARDING RECRUITMENT FRAUD

TE Connectivity has become aware of fraudulent recruitment activities being conducted by individuals or organizations falsely claiming to represent TE Connectivity. Please be advised that TE Connectivity never requests payment or fees from job applicants at any stage of the recruitment process. All legitimate job openings are posted exclusively on our official careers website at te.com/careers, and all email communications from our recruitment team will come only from actual email addresses ending in @te.com. If you receive any suspicious communications, we strongly advise you not to engage or provide any personal information, and to report the incident to your local authorities.

Across our global sites and business units, we put together packages of benefits that are either supported by TE itself or provided by external service providers. In principle, the benefits offered can vary from site to site.

description:
About the job

Job Description

You will work as a part of the Corporate & Investment Bank (CIB) global Strategic Event Marketing (SEM), Sponsorships & Tickets, and Creative Services team, which focuses on strategic direction and content delivery of franchise client events, sponsorships and tickets and creative services. The focus will be Global Investment Banking (GIB) and Payments events in Latin America.

You will engage with a broad range of business partners within SEM as well as with the GIB and Payments businesses and other areas of the firm. The event marketer will assist other team members in strategic execution, plans and protocols, in addition to joining project work streams and data maintenance. The goals are to help GIB and Payments increase client business, deepen existing client relationships, learn to drive efficiencies for all internal partners and maintain the J.P. Morgan brand standard. The role reports to a senior event marketer on the team.

Job Responsibilities


Learn and apply the fundamentals of the business, target audiences and department goals and translate the knowledge into successful client events
Manage planning teams, direct inquiries to the proper individuals or groups, and answer inquiries on events
Work independently on multiple programs and tasks to deliver event-related projects at the same time with different Event Marketers as the lead
Contribute to the planning and execution of events, including, but not limited to: venue research; data maintenance and integrity; internal and external communications; invitation creation, testing and distribution; pre-event tasks, like audio visual and technical requirements; delegation housing and registration; on-site staffing and support; post-event tasks, such as reviewing survey data and supporting debriefs
Responsible for administrative tasks, e.g., manage, review and reconcile reports (RSVP, delegation, registration, dinner assignments, etc.); managing event supplies and shipping; premium research and ordering; and production tasks of event materials like name badges, biographies, speaker introductions, briefing memos and printing
Collaborate with other marketers and other marketing teams on event data and analyses, as well as assist in the development and reconciliation of event budgets
Become a ‘super user' of core event related technologies


Required Qualifications, Capabilities And Skills


Detail-oriented and organized with a strength in project and data management
Proactive, self-sufficient, resourceful and motivated
Strong sense of time management, prioritization and ability to execute against deliverables
Willingness to be a team player and consistently interact with peers, partners, management, relationship managers and clients 
Must be able to tackle any task, trouble shoot and exemplify a positive attitude and commitment to success
BA degree
3+ years of experience in event marketing, preferably in the financial industry
Expert in Microsoft suite, especially Excel (i.e., pivot tables, V lookups and macro is a plus) and PowerPoint (i.e., interactive links, incorporate audio/visual icons, animation); proficient in SharePoint
Proficiency with firm-wide systems or ability to quickly learn systems
Requires early mornings and occasional late nights, as well as some travel


ABOUT US

J.P. Morgan is a global leader in financial services, providing strategic advice and products to the world's most prominent corporations, governments, wealthy individuals and institutional investors. Our first-class business in a first-class way approach to serving clients drives everything we do. We strive to build trusted, long-term partnerships to help our clients achieve their business objectives.

We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants' and employees' religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation.

About The Team

description:
About the job

Please submit your resume in English - we can only consider applications submitted in this language.

Only applications of candidates with Mexican citizenship will be evaluated for this role in compliance with the provisions of Article 7 of the Federal Labor Law.

Minimum qualifications:


Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience.
1 year of experience in business development, partnerships, management consulting, or investment banking, in the consumer electronics, auto, OEMs, telecom, e-commerce/retail, apps, ads, gaming, or technology industries.
Experience working with C-level executives and cross-functionally across all levels of management.
Experience managing agreements or partnerships.
Ability to communicate in Spanish and English fluently to support client relationships in the region.


Preferred qualifications:


Experience working autonomously in a changing, and collaborative environment.
Experience partnering with technology platforms (e.g., CDPs, CRMs, CMSs, data onboarders, etc.), consultancies and agencies.
Experience with Google's performance/measurement products (e.g., data strength, Google tag gateway, data manager, enhanced conversions, vehicle listing ads).
Experience developing AI tools to scale/streamline tasks.
Experience analyzing data to make decisions.


About the jobAs a Community Partner Manager (CPM), you will have the exciting opportunity to engage at scale managing thousands of talented creators across all genres and sizes. You will onboard, educate, engage and support our emerging creator communities across Spanish Speaking Latin America. Your role involves targeted creator onboarding, scalable community communications and engagement, scaled support, and the establishment and execution of a sophisticated operational model including multiple communication platforms and tools, as the team is being run as an innovative hub, and you’ll be a crucial part of the acceleration and adding science to our scale, including through AI. In addition to managing the creators in our "always-on" community, you will be hosting quarterly product-focused bootcamps to drive adoption and scale and to create lighthouse product ambassadors we can partner with for feedback and showcasing the product to other peers.The Global Partnerships organization is responsible for exploring new opportunities with Google's partners. Google’s Global Partnerships team works with a wide range of partners to bring the best of Google to power their business. The Global Partnerships team supports Google’s own Product teams with essential partnerships to help Google’s user experiences in advertising, Search, Assistant, Maps, Travel, Shopping, Payments and more. Teams create product-enabling partnerships, go-to-market strategies and incubate business growth for a variety of products.

Responsibilities


Manage a portfolio of top and emerging creators across Spanish-Speaking Latin America, implementing scaled communication plans tailored to various segments of the creator community.
Design and execute educational and activation programs, including quarterly product-focused bootcamps, scaled workshops, and peer-to-peer sessions (both virtual and in-person) to foster creator development.
Collaborate closely with the Marketing and Product teams to align current trends and campaigns with the creators' content strategies.
Provide quarterly market analysis and execute targeted market action plans to optimize regional performance and ensure a consistent increase in monthly users.
Analyze creator feedback to recommend and drive strategic changes to existing products, policies, and processes, ultimately increasing creator satisfaction, activity, and performance.



Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

About the job

Company

Qualcomm Intl Inc., Mexico Branch Office

Job Area

Sales, Business Development & Marketing Group, Sales, Business Development & Marketing Group > Marketing Communications

General Summary

The Office of the CMO is looking for a Marketing specialist to scale Marketing’s internal engagement, content and communication. This role drives end-to-end program execution across key channels and experiences—including the Global Marketing Summit and All Hands content, learning and development offerings, marketing internship program, and culture initiatives.

The ideal candidate brings strong program management, written skills, a passion for culture-building and learning, and the ability to translate business priorities into engaging content and experiences.  Bachelor's degree and 2+ years of Communications, Marketing, Public Relations, or related work experience.

Capacidad para generar reportes de resultados, interpretar hallazgos clave y convertirlos en acciones para optimizar la estrategia de pauta. Experiencia en análisis de audiencias, segmentación, remarketing, etc.

Experimentar lo que es realmente trabajar en un entorno dinámico, divertido y acelerado.

Dar el siguiente paso en tu carrera profesional junto a un equipo global, productivo y multicultural.

description:
About the job

Role - Campaign Manager - Retail Media

Work Location - Mexico City

Work Mode - OnSite

Work Authorization: Eligible to Work in Mexico




What to expect at MediaMint?

We love people who define their surroundings and who are constantly looking to learn new things. We value honesty and integrity above all. We love people who are honest, self-aware and intent on bettering themselves each day. If you love growth - professional and personal, then MediaMint is most likely the place for you!




What do we do?

MediaMint is an AI-powered Revenue Operations services company dedicated to accelerating innovation and revenue growth for platforms and publishers worldwide. Our mission is to enhance operational efficiency, scalability, customer satisfaction, and data-driven insights through advanced technology, automation, and AI capabilities. We serve industry leaders across AdTech, Consumer, CTV, Publishing, and Retail.




The Role:

As Campaign Manager, you'll own end-to-end ad campaign execution on Client Advertising platforms. This hands-on role delivers seamless launches, optimization, and results across Display, Video, and Audio. Partner with Design, Sales, Ad Ops, and Policy teams to smash targets and boost advertiser success.




What You Bring (2-4 Years Exp):

Proven sales/marketing in e-comm/online ads; global client ownership.
Bachelor's/MBA; superior comms and MS Office (Excel, Word, PPT) skills.
Adaptable in fast-paced ambiguity; quick learner for new tools.




Required Certifications: Amazon Ads Foundations, Sponsored Ads (Basic/Advanced), Video Ads, Retail (Basic/Advanced), Campaign Planning.




Tools: Salesforce, Amazon Ads Console/AMC/Seller Central, MS 365, Slack/Chime.




Please fill this Google form to Apply : Campaign Manager Retail - MX - Onsite




Core Responsibilities: 




Campaign Management & Optimization:

Execute full lifecycle: advertiser onboarding, IO processing, setup, trafficking, troubleshooting
Monitor/optimize daily/weekly for ROAS, CTR, conversions—adjust bids, budgets, targeting
Spot issues, create action plans, and recommend strategies for peak performance.




Data Analysis & Reporting

Pull insights from Amazon Ads, Marketing Cloud (AMC), Seller Central.
Build reports/presentations linking performance to revenue.
Track KPIs and fuel data-driven recommendations.




Cross-Functional Collaboration

Team up with Ad Success, Sales, Vendors, and Product for issue resolution and plan execution.
Escalate blockers and enable solutions.




Sales & Revenue Impact

Hunt growth in accounts; manage pipeline in Salesforce.
Hit activity targets, budget attainment, and SLA response times.
Uphold Amazon SOPs, QA standards, and dispute processes.

skills: Planear, implementar y optimizar campañas de paid media en Google Ads (Search, Display, YouTube, Performance Max), Meta Ads (Facebook e Instagram) y redes de afiliados.

🥳 Un equipo con el que aprender, crecer y disfrutar del camino
description:
About the job

¡Ayúdanos a impulsar el futuro de la tecnología en salud!

En Top Doctors Group trabajamos para mejorar la forma en la que las personas acceden a la atención médica. A través de la tecnología, conectamos pacientes, profesionales sanitarios y clínicas para ofrecer una experiencia más accesible, eficiente y humana.

Hoy estamos presentes en 10 países y desarrollamos soluciones digitales que ayudan a miles de profesionales sanitarios a potenciar su actividad y a millones de personas a encontrar la atención sanitaria que necesitan.

Creemos que la tecnología genera un mayor impacto cuando se pone al servicio de las personas. Por eso seguimos creciendo y buscando talento que quiera ayudarnos a construir el futuro de la salud digital en un entorno internacional, innovador y en constante evolución.

💡 ¿Por qué este rol es interesante?

Porque tendrás un impacto directo en el crecimiento de Top Doctors en Latinoamérica. Como Performance Marketing Specialist, liderarás la estrategia de captación digital de pacientes, médicos y centros médicos, siendo responsable de generar oportunidades de negocio a través de campañas de paid media orientadas a resultados.

Tendrás ownership sobre todo el ciclo de las campañas: desde la definición de la estrategia y la gestión de presupuestos hasta la optimización continua y el análisis de resultados. Trabajarás de forma muy cercana con los equipos de Ventas, Marketing y Revenue para asegurar que los leads generados se conviertan en crecimiento real para el negocio.

Tu trabajo tendrá una influencia directa en métricas clave como la generación de leads, el coste de adquisición, la calidad del pipeline comercial y el retorno de la inversión publicitaria, contribuyendo al crecimiento de una de las compañías HealthTech líderes en Latinoamérica.

🚀 Lo que harás en tu día a día


Planear, implementar y optimizar campañas de paid media en Google Ads (Search, Display, YouTube, Performance Max), Meta Ads (Facebook e Instagram) y redes de afiliados.
Liderar la captación digital de los tres segmentos clave: pacientes, médicos/especialistas y centros médicos o clínicas.
Gestionar y distribuir el presupuesto de inversión publicitaria (media budget) para maximizar el ROI, minimizar el CPA/CAC y escalar la adquisición de forma sostenible.
Dar seguimiento continuo a los leads generados y colaborar con el equipo de Ventas para cerrar el loop de conversión, identificando fricciones y oportunidades de mejora.
Definir, monitorear y reportar los KPIs de performance: CPL, CPC, CPA, ROAS, CAC, tasa de conversión, LTV y revenue atribuido a campañas.
Configurar, mantener y auditar el tracking de conversiones mediante Google Tag Manager, pixels de Meta, eventos de GA4 y herramientas de CRM.
Ejecutar pruebas A/B estructuradas de creatividades, audiencias, segmentaciones y landing pages para mejorar sistemáticamente el rendimiento de las campañas.
Explorar y activar nuevos canales de captación: programmatic advertising, native ads, TikTok Ads, LinkedIn Ads u otros que representen oportunidades para el negocio.
Elaborar reportes periódicos de performance con análisis de resultados, insights accionables y recomendaciones de optimización para la dirección de Marketing.
Colaborar con los equipos de Contenido y Diseño para briefear y validar los activos creativos necesarios para las campañas (banners, videos, landing pages, copies).


👀 Nos encantará conocerte si…

Si disfrutas tomando decisiones basadas en datos, optimizando campañas con impacto real en el negocio y trabajando en entornos donde marketing y revenue están estrechamente conectados, probablemente encajarás muy bien en este rol si tienes:

✔ Entre 3 y 5 años de experiencia en Performance Marketing, Paid Media o Growth Marketing.

✔ Experiencia gestionando campañas de adquisición en Google Ads y Meta Ads de forma autónoma.

✔ Experiencia trabajando con objetivos de negocio vinculados a generación de leads, adquisición de clientes y revenue.

✔ Capacidad para gestionar presupuestos publicitarios y optimizar inversiones en función del ROI.

✔ Dominio avanzado de Google Ads Manager y Meta Ads Manager.

✔ Experiencia avanzada con Google Analytics 4 (GA4) y Google Tag Manager (GTM).

✔ Conocimiento de herramientas de reporting y visualización de datos como Looker Studio, Power BI o similares.

✔ Experiencia utilizando CRM como Salesforce, HubSpot o equivalentes para el seguimiento del ciclo de vida del lead.

✔ Buen manejo de Excel o Google Sheets para análisis de datos y modelización de métricas.

✔ Mentalidad analítica, orientación a resultados y capacidad para convertir datos en acciones concretas.

✔ Español nativo o nivel avanzado e inglés intermedio (B1+) para trabajar con documentación técnica y colaborar con equipos internacionales.

➕ Será un plus contar con certificaciones de Google Ads o Meta Blueprint, experiencia en entornos HealthTech, SaaS o plataformas digitales, y conocimientos de herramientas de CRO o testing como Unbounce o VWO.

🎯 Cómo nos imaginamos a la persona que tendrá éxito en este rol

Más allá de la experiencia técnica, creemos que las personas que mejor encajan en este tipo de posición suelen compartir algunas formas de trabajar:

✔ Toman decisiones basadas en datos y entienden cómo sus acciones impactan en métricas de negocio como el ROI, la rentabilidad y el crecimiento.

✔ Gestionan presupuestos de inversión con criterio, buscando siempre maximizar el impacto de cada euro invertido.

✔ Disfrutan analizando resultados, identificando oportunidades de mejora y optimizando continuamente campañas, procesos y estrategias.

✔ Entienden que el éxito del marketing no termina con la generación de leads, sino que colaboran activamente con los equipos comerciales para mejorar la conversión y la calidad de las oportunidades generadas.

✔ Prestan atención al detalle en aspectos técnicos como el tracking, la medición y la calidad de los datos, asegurando que las decisiones se basen en información fiable.

✔ Se sienten cómodas trabajando en entornos dinámicos, adaptándose con rapidez a los cambios de mercado, nuevas plataformas, algoritmos y comportamientos de los usuarios.

💙 Qué encontrarás al unirte a Top Doctors Group


📈 Posibilidades reales de crecimiento y desarrollo profesional.
💻 Modalidad de trabajo presencial.
🕑 Jornada completa de lunes a viernes de 9:00h a 18:00h
🌎 Un entorno internacional, dinámico y colaborativo
👥 Líderes que priorizan el acompañamiento, la autonomía y el desarrollo de las personas
🥳 Un equipo con el que aprender, crecer y disfrutar del camino


🌱 Diversidad e inclusión

En Top Doctors Group creemos que la diversidad de experiencias, perspectivas y trayectorias nos ayuda a construir mejores equipos y mejores soluciones. Trabajamos para construir un entorno diverso, inclusivo y donde todas las personas tengan las mismas oportunidades para desarrollarse profesionalmente. Si necesitas cualquier adaptación o apoyo durante el proceso de selección, estaremos encantados de ayudarte.

🚀 ¿Te interesa?

Si has llegado hasta aquí y te identificas en buena parte con lo que buscamos, nos encantará conocerte. No creemos en las candidaturas perfectas ni en los perfiles que encajan al 100% sobre el papel, más allá de la experiencia concreta, valoramos aquellas competencias, habilidades y formas de trabajar que permiten aportar valor al equipo y contribuir al crecimiento de la compañía. Si crees que este reto puede encajar contigo, ¡aplica ahora!

title: Marketing Transformation (Adobe) Consultant - Mexico
description:
About the job

Join Our Global Consulting Network and Expand Your Horizons!

Are you ready to take your career to new heights? At the Global Network, we are more than just a team; we are a thriving community of over 10,000 professionals operating from key locations around the world. We specialize in providing top-tier consulting expertise across various industries and functional areas, including life sciences, energy, supply chain operations, finance, and talent management.

Why Choose Us?

Global Exposure: Work on international projects and gain invaluable insights into diverse cultures and business practices.

Specialized Development: We don’t just work across broad spectrums; we ensure you develop deep expertise in specific areas of management consulting, enhancing both your professional value and expertise.

Dynamic Work Environment: Our work is fast-paced and challenging, pushing you to grow quickly and continuously improve. It’s not just about meeting expectations; it’s about exceeding them and making a significant impact.

Innovation at the Core: At the Global Network, innovation isn't just a buzzword—it's a fundamental part of how we operate. We leverage cutting-edge technologies and innovative strategies to solve complex problems and deliver novel solutions that keep our clients ahead in a rapidly changing world.

We are driven by a commitment to excellence and a passion for delivering results that matter. If you are ambitious, talented, and ready for a challenge, we want to hear from you. Reach out, join our network, and start a conversation that could lead to your next big opportunity.

Let’s build a better future together!

Job Summary:

As a Marketing and Customer Analytics Consultant, you will lead the design, implementation, and optimization of analytics solutions for clients. Your role will involve conducting user experience assessments, managing data processes, and delivering actionable insights to improve marketing initiatives and customer journeys.

Roles & Responsibilities:



Conduct user experience assessments on websites/apps and provide recommendations to optimize analytics stacks.

Gather business requirements and translate complex functional, technical, and business needs into architectural designs and solution blueprints.

Manage processes for capturing, storing, using, and tracking online activity data from multiple channels.

Use consultative skills, business knowledge, and technical expertise to integrate analytics technologies into clients’ digital platforms and achieve business results.

Analyze integration aspects between web analytics and allied systems like websites, apps, campaign managers, audience managers, Firebase, and tag managers.

Plan and design digital analytics/clickstream data inclusion in customer 360 views and provide frameworks, solutions, architectures, and roadmaps for clients.

Understand customer journeys and current trends to provide recommendations for improving marketing initiatives, personalization, and advertising strategies.

Identify, scope, and partner with technology solution providers and marketing agencies to deliver tailored solutions.

Work with technology teams to orchestrate and implement integrations, including API-based integrations.

Understand and convey the impact of regulations like HIPAA, CCPA, and GDPR on marketing channels.

Mobilize and organize across multiple stakeholders and projects with strong project management skills.

Drive business development efforts and thought leadership in the digital marketing platform space.



Professional & Technical Skills:



Must Have Skills: website/app and marketing analytics tools like Adobe Analytics (Priority), Adobe Target, Google Analytics, Adobe CJA, NetInsight, Appsflyer, WebTrends, Optimizely, SEMRush, and Firebase.

Hands-on experience in delivering and executing technical/functional design solutions.

Good to Have Skills: Experience with tag management platforms (GTM, Adobe Launch, Tealium), Ads conversion pixels, DMPs, and visualization tools like Tableau, PowerBI, and Google Data Studio.



Your experience counts!



Bachelor’s degree completed; MBA is highly desirable.

Advanced level of English and excellent oral and written communication skills 

+5 years of proven working experience in developing and executing data-driven marketing, web, and customer analytics across industries such as E-Commerce, CPG, Telecom, Healthcare, and Financial Services.

Experience in projects with Adobe Platforms.

Strong understanding of collaboration between web/app analytics and other marketing, advertising, and personalization technologies.

Preferred but not mandatory: Experience working with Ads Data Hub, ad platforms, and their APIs (e.g., Google API, Facebook Conversion API).

Hands-on functional consulting experience preferably in Adobe marketing processes, campaign lifecycles, and integration with other MarTech platforms implementation with deep functional, industry and client facing experience

Good consulting, techno-functional and project management experience is preferred.

Availability to travel may be required.

description:
About the job

This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Paid Online Retail & Marketplace Ops Specialist based in Mexico.

This role focuses on managing and optimizing paid retail media campaigns across major online marketplaces while ensuring strong operational performance across product listings and seller platforms. You will be responsible for driving advertising efficiency through data-driven optimization of PPC campaigns, keyword strategies, and performance budgets. The position blends hands-on campaign management with marketplace operations, including catalog accuracy, pricing, compliance, and inventory alignment. You will collaborate closely with marketing, sales, and merchandising teams to ensure advertising strategies support broader e-commerce objectives. The role requires strong analytical thinking and a deep understanding of retail media ecosystems. It is ideal for a detail-oriented professional who thrives in fast-paced, performance-driven e-commerce environments.

Accountabilities


Plan, launch, and optimize retail media advertising campaigns across marketplaces such as Home Depot, Lowe’s, Wayfair, Costco, and Sam’s Club, including Sponsored Products, Sponsored Brands, display, and video formats.
Manage end-to-end marketplace operations, including product listings, content updates, keyword optimization, pricing adjustments, compliance checks, and inventory alignment.
Monitor and analyze campaign performance KPIs such as CTR, ROAS, ACoS, and conversion rates, providing actionable insights for continuous optimization.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams including sales, marketing, and merchandising to align paid media strategies with overall e-commerce goals.
Generate regular performance reports and dashboards to track ad spend, ROI, and operational efficiency, supporting data-driven decision-making.
Troubleshoot listing and campaign issues within seller platforms and ensure smooth operational execution across multiple marketplaces.


Requirements


3+ years of experience managing retail media or e-commerce advertising campaigns across platforms such as Amazon Advertising, Walmart Connect, Wayfair, Home Depot Retail Media, or similar.
Strong understanding of PPC structures, including keyword targeting, bidding strategies, budget management, and A/B testing.
Experience working with marketplace backend systems such as Wayfair Partner Home, Mirakl-based platforms, or equivalent seller tools.
Strong analytical skills with proficiency in Excel or Google Sheets and experience using reporting dashboards to optimize performance.
High attention to detail with the ability to manage product catalogs, listings, and compliance requirements accurately.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English.
Ability to work full-time in the CST time zone.
Experience in agency environments or managing multiple brands is a plus.


Benefits


Competitive salary aligned with experience and market standards.
Fully remote work model with flexibility.
Opportunity to work across major global retail marketplaces.
Exposure to high-scale e-commerce advertising operations.
Professional development in retail media, analytics, and marketplace strategy.
Collaborative and performance-driven remote team environment.
Opportunity to manage complex, multi-platform campaigns.

skills: Update and publish content across Sitecore XM, HubSpot, and Sitecore Content Hub, ensuring accuracy, accessibility, and brand alignment.

description:
About the job

Company Introduction

WorldStrides is the global leader in educational travel and experiential learning. The company was founded in 1967 to provide middle school travel programs to Washington, D.C. and has grown to provide a wide range of programs for more than half a million students annually to over 100 countries around the world. WorldStrides offers experiential learning programs in educational travel, performing arts, language immersion, career exploration, service-learning, study abroad, and sports. Each of these experiences helps students to see beyond the classroom and to see the world – and themselves – in new ways.

Job Description

At WorldStrides, we’re passionate about connecting students with life-changing global experiences. As our Digital Experience Specialist, you’ll help ensure those stories are discoverable, engaging, and data-driven—turning insights into action and visitors into explorers. Reporting to the Vice President of Marketing, you’ll maintain and enhance our digital presence for the Higher Education division. In this data-driven role, you’ll create, manage, and optimize website content while monitoring site analytics to identify opportunities that drive engagement, conversions, and SEO performance. You’ll collaborate with marketing, creative, and technical teams to ensure our web experience is both inspiring and effective.

Responsibilities

Website Management & Content Execution


Update and publish content across Sitecore XM, HubSpot, and Sitecore Content Hub, ensuring accuracy, accessibility, and brand alignment.
Build and maintain new landing pages and microsites that support campaigns and key initiatives.
Optimize page structure, layout, and assets (including images and metadata) for SEO and performance.
Conduct periodic audits to fix broken links, outdated information, or publish errors.


Analytics & Performance Insights


Use HubSpot, Google Analytics 4 (GA4), and BrightEdge to monitor site traffic, engagement, and conversion metrics.
Translate analytical findings into actionable recommendations to improve user flow and content strategy.
Collaborate with marketing teams to measure campaign effectiveness and report on key KPIs.
Maintain and refine dashboards, tagging, and event tracking through Google Tag Manager.


Search Engine Optimization


Monitor SEO diagnostics and implement on-page optimizations to improve visibility and search performance.
Work with content creators to integrate relevant keywords and follow SEO best practices.
Ensure technical SEO elements—such as redirects, alt text, canonical tags, and structured data—are consistently applied.


Continuous Improvement


Identify trends in content performance, UX behavior, and user intent to guide enhancements.
